title: 10 Simple Ways To Prepare Your Home or Condo To Sell
h1: 10 Simple Ways To Prepare Your Home To Sell
meta: 10 Simple Ways To Prepare Your Home To Sell

blocks.text: Here are 10 simple ways to prepare your home or condo to sell and stand out from the masses. You've thought long and hard, worked to get logistics in line and you're finally ready to list your home to sell. Preparing your home to enter the real estate market can feel overwhelming and new. There are several options to keep in mind that will optimize your chances of selling fast and for a higher price. The real estate market ebbs and flows, so preparing your home and doing everything that you can to make it stand out is crucial. Staged homes are reported to sell for 18% more than a home that isn’t staged and spend an average of only 11 days or less on the market. We have seen it time after time, staging your home will help your home to sell. Hiring a professional stager is the best option, especially if your home or condo is currently unfurnished. However, if you're listing your home and plan on making the changes yourself, there are simple tricks you can do on your own to prepare your home for the market.
1. Increase Curb Appeal

Most buyers won't anticipate seeing the interior of the home if the exterior isn't appealing. Ensure that the mailbox is clean, stands straight, and that the house numbers are clear. Landscaping plays a significant role in the overall appearance of a home. Trim trees, pull weeds, and plant new flowers. Replace old droopy plants with fresh lively greens. As buyers arrive you want them to feel welcomed and visualize pulling into the home as if it were their own. Arriving for the first time in a yard that needs love is a sure way to cause them stress thinking about work that already needs to be done.
2. Give It a Deep Cleaning

Every home should be thoroughly cleaned before going on the market. Scrub windows, wipe down baseboards, clean the floors, and all the hard-to-reach places that often get overlooked. Buyers will look in the bathrooms, make sure that tile is bright and clean! It's a great time to go through unused household items, closets, and drawers. Keep in mind the more you do prior to the sale, the less you'll have to do once it's moving time. A good cleanout brings organization to daily life tasks, creates a sense of peace of mind, and makes the moving process simpler.
3. Clear Clutter

Clutter can make a home feel dirty and small. Clear the tabletops, kitchen counters, and other surfaces. Papers, pens, magazines, newspapers, and other objects can easily be well organized into a proper place. Move small kitchen appliances such as blenders and toasters into lower cabinets. Clear the refrigerator of photos, artwork, and magnets. Closets will be peeked in, so make sure things are tidy. Home buyers will often assume there is a lack of storage space if they see packed closets, drawers, and shelves. Laundry rooms and entryways are easy collection areas. Make sure that laundry baskets are emptied or stored neatly in a closet. Put clothing, shoes, and detergent bottles away. For a nice touch in the laundry room, add a stack of neatly folded fresh white towels.
4. Think White & Bright

Pure white linens look clean, fresh, and photograph well. Staging your home with white creates a blank slate, helping potential buyers imagine decorating the space as their own. This means switching out old towels that are bold in color, patterned, or dingy for a set of pure white or neutral-colored towels. The same rule applies to the beds, a white bed is inviting and clean. Guests can better imagine their own bedding in the environment. We recommend a natural linen-like duvet cover in white.
5. Let The Light In

Great lighting is essential in making a home look and feels inviting. Pull back curtains and open up blinds to allow natural light to flow in. Turn on lighting fixtures and make adjustments in dim-lit places. When the lights are on it shows that you have nothing to hide. Buyers can see paint, flooring, and room size better when the home is well lit. Light-colored walls reflect light making a room feel larger and brighter, so you may consider trading dark and bold colored walls for white or light gray. We suggest Chantilly Lace by Benjamin Moore or City Loft by Sherwin Williams. These two colors go well will pre-existing decor and will help create a blank canvas for buyers.
6. Make Repairs

A home that is move-in ready will have a greater appeal to buyers. Take an inventory of what needs to be done and fix what you can prior to showing your home. Small repairs will cause buyers to question if there are larger repairs needed. The truth is, even the best cared for homes will have at least a few issues in their lifetime. But leaving these issues for the next homeowners to solve can be a major deterrent to potential home buyers. As they tour the home they're most likely calculating repair costs and those calculations will affect the offer.
7. De-personalize

Tuck away personal items such as family photos, paperwork, children's artwork, and bathroom items. You want the buyer to imagine their own things in the space. While these are beautiful things to have in your home, they can distract buyers and make some of them feel uncomfortable. This is crucial in helping the buyers avoid feeling like they're walking through a stranger's home.
8. Have a Plan For Pets & Children

By limiting the sign of pets and children, you'll appeal to the highest number of buyers. For children, neaten bedrooms by tucking away toys and brightly colored decor. You can neutralize their rooms by organizing their toys into attractive storage bins or baskets. Hide pet food and water dishes, leashes, beds, and toys. Finding a dog sitter may not be easy, but it will pay off. It's difficult to have a serene walk-through with a barking dog or cat lounging on furniture. You also want to keep in mind allergies. Vacuuming before each showing will eliminate or lower the chances of buyers noticing pet hair and debris. Walk the lawn to check for any messes or damage left by pets.
9. Make The Bed Every Day

Making the bed is a very simple step, but it makes a tremendous difference. Ensure that all the beds are made and wrinkle-free before the home is shown. An unmade, messy bed can make a room feel cluttered and unattractive. Extra neutral-colored pillows, a throw, and sometimes even a tray with a small stack of books or a couple of glasses can add an inviting touch.
10. Set The Table

The Dining room is a place to gather, enjoy a great meal and spend time with friends. It's a place where everyday rituals take place and where memories are made. Clear the dining table of keys, paperwork, and miscellaneous objects. Set the table with placemats, plates, stemware, and a simple centerpiece or fresh flowers. Creating a dinner scene will help the potential buyer use their imagination.

Remember that staging and preparing your home is essentially merchandising it to sell. This doesn't mean you have poor taste in decor, it simply means that you have to create a clean, well-put-together home that is ready for someone else to picture it as their own. Renting a storage unit throughout the showing process is a great way to keep things that you need while allowing you to simplify your home so it's ready to be shown and ultimately, sell. So, if you're a homeowner in South Florida and you're considering putting your house on the market we'd love to help you prepare. Compass Concierge takes care of staging from start to finish, making sure that your home is beautiful, and ready for photos, and showings.

title: 2023 Interior Design Trends
h1: 2023 Interior Design Trends
meta: 7 Interior Design trends you'll wan't to incorporate in your home in 2023.

blocks.text: Interior design trends are quickly changing in 2023. Homeowners of today are looking for designs that are both stylish and practical, with a focus on personality, comfort, and sustainability. Here are some of the current interior design trends for homeowners in 2023, whether you're renovating your current home or seeking ideas for a new one.
Sustainable Practices and Materials

An increasing number of homeowners are searching for ways to make their homes eco-friendly as awareness of environmental issues grows. This involves utilizing eco-friendly materials and practices in their interior design. We can expect an emphasis on energy-efficient lighting and appliances, in addition to an increase in the usage of materials like bamboo, repurposed wood, and recycled glass. In order to create a healthier and more sustainable living environment, designers are also turning to biophilic design, which includes natural features like plants and sunlight.
Vibrant Hues, Patterns, and Accents

Homeowners are adopting bold colors and patterns in 2023 to give their homes character and flair but in moderation. To make a statement with their decor, homeowners are opting for a mix of bold walls to vibrant furniture and accessories. Warm neutrals like terracotta and rust, as well as jewel tones like emerald green and sapphire blue, are popular color choices for 2023. Interior designer Anne Hepfer says “Ultramarine blue will be the trending color for the year…” In terms of patterns, there are many, we are seeing geometric shapes, bold florals, and abstract designs. “Stripes are going to come back in a big way. They’re the original high-contrast design element, whether used in wallpaper, textiles, or paint, and make an unmistakably bold statement, unlike any other pattern,” says Carrie Livingston, Interior Designer.
Simplicity and Minimalism

Even though vivid colors and patterns are hot, homeowners will still favor minimalism and simplicity in 2023. This style emphasizes clean lines, straightforward shapes, warm woods, and a muted color palette. Neutral hues, natural materials like wood and stone, and an emphasis on efficiency over decoration are frequent features of minimalist interiors. Those who are downsizing or want a simpler, more minimalist lifestyle are particularly fond of this design trend.
Coziness and Comfort

Many homeowners are prioritizing comfort and coziness while designing their interior spaces. This comprises luxurious, supple textiles, cozy furnishings, and cozy lighting. To provide a sense of coziness and familiarity to their living spaces, many homeowners are now including personal touches like artwork, heirlooms, and family photos.
Various Metals

Mixing metals is becoming a common design motif for homes in 2023. This entails combining various metal finishes, such as copper, brass, gold, and silver, in a single area. This gives a space a sense of depth and visual intrigue while still maintaining a consistent aesthetic. Combining diverse textures and finishes into a room using metals is another excellent method to give it character and warmth. Gold over brushed nickel is making a comeback.
Organic Forms and Curved Shapes

For 2023, organic forms and curved shapes are popular, and many designers are using rounded edges and gentle curves in their designs. This includes rounded mirrors, curved furniture, and organically shaped objects like vases and bowls. These delicate, fluid shapes give a space a sense of movement and fluidity while also adding a dash of sophistication and beauty.
Technological Fusion

Many homeowners are looking for methods to incorporate technology into their interior design as it continues to play a larger role in our lives. This includes high-tech appliances, wireless charging stations, and smart home systems. More designs that smoothly integrate technology into the home are anticipated in 2023, which will make life and work simpler and more convenient.

The 2023 interior design trends for homes show a desire for comfort, personal expression, and sustainability. There is a design trend to fit every preference and aesthetic, from daring hues and patterns to minimalism and simplicity. Whether you are updating your current space or bringing a completely new design into a new home, we are sure you’ll find a trend that suits your style.

title: 4 Best Designed Restaurants in Brickell
h1: 4 Best Designed Restaurants in Brickell
meta: The Best Designed Restaurants in Brickell. If you love Miami and love bites and environments that tantalize the eyes and the palette this is for you.

blocks.text: It’s no secret Miami has become a must-visit city for its vibrant culinary scene. But it’s not just the flavors that have foodies wanting to book a resy. With highly sought-after flavors comes a tribe of designers/architects whose jobs are to ensure the vibe and concept of each restaurant are up to par with the presentation of every dish.

From newcomers on the scene to swank, sophisticated outposts that have garnered A-listers weekly, we’re breaking down Brickell’s top four restaurants with the best interior design. At these hot spots, what’s on your plate is just as impressive as the surroundings.
Dirty French Steakhouse

In the heart of Miami’s Brickell neighborhood lies the new, hottest steakhouse: Dirty French, featuring the best meat and seafood selections with a touch of French flare. This culinary gem is an over-the-top, animal-print-obsessed eatery envisioned by the one-and-only Ken Fulk. It’s just another reason to visit Miami—or another reason to call Brickell home.

Dirty French’s concept eludes an 80s-era swagger that only Miami’s glamorous scene can fully enjoy. Before dinner, head to the Jungle Bar for a tasty tipple like the Ziggy Stardust and sit on one of the fringed cheetah print bar stools while admiring the custom leaf-patterned walls and Murano glass leaf chandelier.

From there, dine inside one of a handful of dining rooms, each boasting its own playful character. The main room is dimly lit and boasts zebra print dining chairs. Steps away is a private dining parlor inclusive of plum banquettes for more intimate gatherings. One of Dirty French’s dining rooms even features floor-to-ceiling mirrored disco ball walls, colored mirrored tables, and a Ken Fulk–designed rug in collaboration with Pierre Frey.

This best-designed restaurant was conceived by the globally renowned hospitality group Major Food Group (MFG), which operates a handful of popular concepts including Carbone and ZZ’s Club. They will debut a new residential living community soon for those who can’t get enough of their sophisticated concepts and want to make it part of their lifestyle.
The Lowdown: In the heart of Brickell, Dirty French Steakhouse is accessible to many of the area’s hottest residences including Echo Brickell, Lofty Brickell, and the up-and-coming MAJOR Residences.
Hutong

The next best-designed restaurant on our Brickell list hails from Hong Kong and brings a fresh take to upscale Chinese cuisine. Hutong made its debut in 2019 and marks the brand’s second U.S. outpost following its New York locale.

There’s plenty to love at Hutong, including its expansive menu offering everything from truffle mushroom bao buns to Zhen Jiang pork ribs to Hutong Dan Dan Noodles (Sichuan classic dish served in a spicy minced pork, sesame, and peanut broth). But, the interiors take this culinary experience to the next level with traditional Chinese design elements mixed with Miami art influences, making this one stunningly designed restaurant not to miss.

The breathtaking atmosphere, designed by Norberto Rosenstein Architect Inc., has all the wow factors. First, 35,000 antique grey bricks expand to the tops of the restaurant’s high ceilings, which are said to be hand-chiseled and were transported all the way from a Chinese building built in the 1930s. There’s a “Great Wall” of hand-carved wood panels that pulsate in light and color when the restaurants bring in the hottest DJs, and every other inch features an immaculate design element you have to see to believe. Head to this outpost for lunch, dinner, and two-hour Bubbles and Bao brunch.
The Lowdown: Hutong’s locale makes it an ideal dinner hangout for those living in Brickell proper such as at Brickell Flatiron, or close-by in downtown at Paramount Miami Worldcenter.
Sexy Fish

Get ready to dine at one of the newest, over-the-top Brickell haunts with Sexy Fish. Designed by Martin Brudnizki Design Studio, this design-forward outpost is the sister concept to Richard Caring’s lauded Sexy Fish London and sits on Miami’s famed Brickell Avenue.

Say what you want about the food—many out-of-towners and locals alike come simply to admire the glamorous and opulent artworks by Damien Hirst, plus an insane installation by celebrated architect and designer Frank Gehry, whose 26 fish lamps play and sway to the overall concept.

Between bites of Wagyu Tataki and Chilean sea bass sneak a peek at the restaurant’s grandeur, which even extends to the ornate bathrooms. The women’s restroom carries the underwater theme with mermaid tail sinks, while the men’s room features a cameo by James Bond with a life-like sculpture of Daniel Craig in scuba gear. It’s one aqua fantasy that keeps on giving.

The Lowdown: Surrounded by Brickell’s hottest nightlife, Sexy Fish is sure to become a weekly dinner (or lunch go-to), especially for those who live close by in the Baccarat Residences or Cipriani Residences,
Komodo

We would be remiss if we didn’t include one of downtown Miami’s most famed restaurants on this best-designed Brickell restaurant list. While it’s not new, Komodo still ranks as one of the best Miami restaurants in town—and it’s not just because of the food. Foodies come to enjoy a vibe-heavy atmosphere underscored by a design inspired by the Komodo dragon native to Southeast Asia. New York–based ICRAVE is behind the floating, tropical oasis that takes over two floors of space.

Focal points range from 40-foot ceilings dotted with oversized bamboo rods and illuminated metals. The room is sultry red and gives off a treehouse vibe visible via five floating bird’s nest pods, which are home to several private dining spaces. Outside is just as stunning with lush gardens that play into the Asian-themed design.

The Lowdown: Locals and soon-to-be residents will definitely make Komodo part of their weekly rotation after one visit. With its close proximity to downtown, this restaurant is easily accessible even to those a little farther north.

title: 7 Ways to Make Your Home More Eco-Friendly
h1: Eco-Friendly Living
meta:
7 Ways to Make Your Home More Eco-Friendly 


It’s no secret that our world is undergoing rapid and undeniable change, including the environment we adore....

blocks.text: It’s no secret that our world is undergoing rapid and undeniable change, including the environment we adore. Nowhere is this more evident than in our dynamic South Florida landscape. With rising temperatures, we face more powerful storm damage, plenty of hot days ahead, and the harming of our precious marine life. Despite this, hope is on the horizon. As stewards of our slice of paradise, we can make a positive impact. By embracing eco-conscious practices within our homes, we can create a ripple effect of change that safeguards our unique ecosystem and enhances our quality of life.
What Are Eco-Friendly Homes?

You might wonder, "What is an eco-friendly home?" If solar panels immediately come to mind, you are on the right track (more on that later). Eco-friendly homes are ingeniously designed to promote greener living, effectively reducing the emission of greenhouse gasses into the atmosphere. These homes combine sustainable materials and cutting-edge technologies, leading to lower energy consumption and reduced water usage for homeowners.
Why Go Eco-Friendly?

Eco-friendly houses are part of a growing sustainable trend that enhances energy efficiency and shrinks our carbon footprint. These environmentally conscious homes offer a more secure future for our planet and noticeable reductions in utility bills. Beyond that, adopting a more eco-friendly lifestyle results in lower maintenance needs, improved air quality, reduced expenses, and more. It's important to note that while new eco-friendly homes might require a higher initial investment (1%-11% more in construction costs), the long-term benefits far outweigh this upfront expense.
The Future of Homebuilding

The shift toward eco-friendly practices in home construction is gaining momentum. There’s an Energy and Environmental Building Alliance (EEBA) group of 72,000 builders, architects, and other stakeholders across North America collaborating on how to build homes more sustainably. Zero-energy homes and apartments are diminishing reliance on conventional energy sources while fostering self-sufficiency.

The appeal goes beyond sustainability; there's also a considerable return on investment. According to a recent study, a home with an EnergyStar rating can boost its value by 9 percent. Homes with energy-saving and sustainable features command higher prices and create high demand among buyers.
7 Tips for Creating an Eco-Friendly Home

While there are many ways to improve your home, here are seven of the easiest methods to consider. Keep these tips top of mind, and you'll be one step closer to a sustainable future that benefits you and the environment.
1. Install Energy- and Water-Efficient Appliances

Households continue to play a significant role in producing harmful greenhouse gasses due to their extensive use of energy-consuming appliances. Start by investing in energy- and water-efficient appliances, like dryers, washers, refrigerators, freezers, and air purifiers. You’ll not only save money on your water bill but also reduce your carbon footprint.
2. Buy a Smart Thermostat

What is a smart thermostat? A smart thermostat, equipped with Wi-Fi connectivity, optimizes heating and cooling temperatures for optimal performance. Beyond simple adjustments, you can tailor settings to your work schedule, frequented areas within your home, and preferred temperature settings for different times of the day. You can even program your system to activate and deactivate based on your timetable, weather conditions, and the number of people at home.
3. Use Recycled, Renewable, and Locally Sourced Building Materials

Selecting sustainable materials leads to less waste compared to conventional options like plastic. This choice results in fewer items in landfills, reduced energy consumption, and a smaller overall environmental impact. Consider making tweaks with the flooring you choose or cabinetry in your kitchen.
4. Properly Insulate Your Home

A well-insulated home offers lasting advantages. It minimizes heat loss, reduces energy expenses, enhances comfort, and decreases the environmental impact. Opt for eco-friendly insulation materials like sheep's wool, cork, cellulose, and Icynene, and ensure the products are manufactured using energy-efficient processes.
5. Install Solar Panels

We’re all familiar with the rise of solar panels. They’ve become a more common sight around town at places such as Oceanfront Beach Park; you might have even seen them adorning rooftops in neighborhoods. That’s because solar panels harness the sun’s energy into power. As South Florida boasts ample sunlight, adding solar panels to your home reduces reliance on traditional electricity sources, favoring power generated directly from your rooftop. On average, solar panel installation ranges from $11,050 to $14,950 in Florida.

For an even simpler option, consider the FPL SolarTogether program, available to all FPL customers in Florida, no matter if you rent or own your home or condo. This alternative relies on Florida-based solar energy centers, eliminating the need for rooftop panels.
6. Be Mindful of Landscaping Materials

One thing that might go unnoticed when you become a homeowner is how much you’ll invest in landscaping. Instead of choosing plants that only look good, try planting varieties native to our Florida landscape. This will require less maintenance and water and keep the plants healthier over time. Did you know 500 square feet of lawn can take up about 67,500 gallons of water annually? Also, avoid using chemicals or pesticides where possible, as the toxins enter our water supply and contaminate it. These chemicals pose risks to the environment, our health, our pets, and other wildlife.
7. Sustainable Windows

Invest in sustainable windows with a low-emissivity (Low-E) glass coating, reflecting heat indoors during winter and away during summer. Triple-pane or double-pane windows are strongly recommended for peak energy efficiency in various climate zones. The insulation between window panes conserves energy and lowers electricity costs.

While these seven tips merely scratch the surface of available options, the possibilities are boundless. As highlighted, incorporating practical strategies like energy-efficient appliances, solar panel utilization, and insulation optimization lays the foundation for a more sustainable future for both South Floridians and beyond. Consider starting here and seeking out even more ways to become more eco-friendly in the future.

title: A New Wave of Coastal Living at Olara West Palm Beach
h1: A New Wave of Coastal Living at Olara West Palm Beach
meta: Olara West Palm Beach is one of the newest New-Construction residential buildings coming to West Palm Beach. This luxury residence will be the start of luxury residences along the North Flagler corridor.

blocks.text: It may seem like Miami is at the forefront of the residential development boom in South Florida. But Palm Beach County is quickly on the rise. With its high demand for condo living, an influx of luxury residences are going up rapidly. One of the newest and most exclusive offerings is Olara West Palm Beach, a luxury waterfront residential oasis in The Palm Beaches.

Dubbed “a haven for effortless living,” Olara’s 26 floors of two- to four-bedroom plus den residences feature exquisitely designed indoor and outdoor spaces overlooking the Intracoastal, complemented by a holistic amenity suite on the sixth floor. New York–based real estate investment and development firm Savanna is behind the new condominium whose 80,000 square feet of property allow future homeowners to enjoy every luxury experience that makes the Palm Beach lifestyle oh-so alluring.

“South Florida is an iconic destination, and its popularity — especially among New Yorkers — is continuing to grow,” shares Chris Schlank, President and Co-Chairman of Savanna. “We decided to focus on West Palm Beach because of the city’s incredible potential… we feel that our project Olara will spur a luxury transformation.”

Olara also marks the first time a five-star condominium project will debut on North Flagler. “It is poised to change expectations for the area,” he says. “North Flagler is on track to become the nation’s next major corridor for the live-work-play lifestyle.”
Read along as we uncover this new waterfront gem distinguished by a private marina, unparalleled amenities, and more.
Quick Facts: Olara West Palm Beach
Over two acres of resort-inspired indoor and outdoor amenity spaces
30,000-square-foot, celebrity-chef–helmed restaurant
Private marina located along the Intracoastal
Olara Residences

Savanna enlisted help from the renowned architecture firm Arquitectonica to create the residences, which range from two- to four-bedrooms plus a den. The building is inspired by the water and fluidity found near the Olara community. Interior design firm Gabellini Sheppard Associates will assist in creating elegant and effortless interiors, each detail refreshing and designed with well-being in mind.

“Our vision was to honor the water in which Olara overlooks,” says Schlank. “Floor-to-ceiling windows, expansive terraces, and soft architectural curves maximize the views and ensure residents feel connected to the natural environment. From the European-style, gourmet kitchens to the spa-like dual water closets, the lifestyle was thoughtfully designed to be a personal paradise in South Florida.”
Here’s what to expect at Olara West Palm Beach:
Floor-to-ceiling windows that open to 10-foot-deep terraces
European-style gourmet kitchen with marble countertops, backsplash, and island
Full-size, climate-controlled butler closets
Dual water closets and spa-inspired bathrooms
Olara At Your Service

Olara’s interiors alone are enough to make you want to live here, but it’s the warm hospitality and meticulous attention to detail that makes this a living experience like none other. A 24-hour doorman plus valet and porter services are available to Olara residents. There’s a dedicated resident manager and concierge to help you with everything — nothing is ever too large or small.

When you work up an appetite, enjoy meals either via catering by a to-be-named chef-helmed eatery or utilize the in-home dining service on the property. The condo also created a dedicated Olara app for residents so that everything is at their fingertips.
Exceptional Amenities at Olara West Palm Beach

Just when you thought Olara couldn’t get any better — it does with its unparalleled indoor and outdoor amenities, all located on a private sixth-floor deck. The project’s nearly 80,000 square feet of amenities were designed like a five-star resort, providing a “well-rounded, hassle-free lifestyle” to those who will reside at the tower, says Schlank.
From health and wellness to leisurely pursuits, there’s something for everyone. Take a look:
Health & Wellness

The details announced for Olara’s health-related amenities begin with a world-class fitness center designed by The Wright Fit and end with a Japanese-inspired Onsen (hot springs naturally occurring in Japan due to its volcanic state).

And there’s so much more in between — 13,000 square feet of indoor and outdoor fitness spaces, including an open-air yoga deck, performance training zones with indoor-outdoor turf lanes, a calming energy studio for - yoga, mat Pilates, and breath work. Enjoy sauna, steam, and treatment rooms plus cold plunge, vitality pools, and an ice fountain to reinvigorate your senses.
Leisure & Entertainment

For those who prefer a little more stimulation, head to the waterfront veranda and library, or multimedia lounge to enjoy the newest flicks and TV shows. Take a dip in the leisure pool and spa, swim some laps, and relax in one of the poolside cabanas. Residents can also reserve the private dining room to host friends and family, and make use of the catering kitchen. Nautical lovers will find a private marina with six boat slips available for use. Schlank also notes a bevy of seafaring experiences will be available to future residents with activities like snorkeling and day charters for fishing and more.
Local Love Near Olara West Palm Beach

Aside from Olara’s wow-factor lifestyle on the property, residents will be able to enjoy all there is to offer around town, just minutes from the building. “Olara is a well-positioned, four-acre modern oasis with close proximity to world-class shopping, dining, and white-sand beaches,” says Schlank. “The location on the Intracoastal, as well as the building’s private marina and exciting epicurean marketplace and restaurant, will set a new standard for the neighborhood.”

From Palm Beach Island’s picturesque shopping and dining destinations to Downtown West Palm Beach, The Square, nightlife on Clematis Street and entertainment at The Kravis Center, there’s something for everyone to enjoy.

We can’t forget to mention the many outdoor wonders of Palm Beach County. Expansive beaches, Loggerhead Marinelife Center, and miles of ground to cover in natural parks will keep you exploring all year long.
Olara is also located near the West Palm Beach Brightline station, so you can travel with ease to surrounding South Florida counties. A day trip to Fort Lauderdale or Miami has never been easier!

Schlank’s final message for us was “Olara is forging the way for new growth in the area of North Flagler. In a sense, we are creating the blueprint for tomorrow. We’re proud to have fostered great partnerships with community leaders who also believe in our vision to transform West Palm Beach into a top-world destination for prime real estate.”

Learn more about Olara West Palm Beach and discover how to make this waterfront oasis your new home. Want to speak to an Olara specialist about potentially purchasing one of the one-of-a-kind residences click here.

title: A Taste of Prime Waterfront Living at Alba Palm Beach
h1: Waterfront Condos at ALBA Palm Beach
meta: Alba Palm Beach is introducing a limited-edition waterfront lifestyle that is hard to come by, get a taste of prime waterfront living at Alba Palm Beach. This interview with the development team behind Alba Palm Beach sheds light on what we can expec

blocks.text: While many developers are opting for a “bigger is better” mentality in South Florida especially when it comes to waterfront condos (think: towering highrises dotting every open lot spanning the Tri-County area), BGI Companies—along with co-developer Blue Road Group—are slowing things down with a brand-new residential project: Alba Palm Beach.

The teams behind the new development in Northwood say Alba will “redefine waterfront living in The Palm Beaches,” thanks to 55 intimately sized, graciously appointed residences plus a variety of amenities on the sixth floor to boot. These will include virtual conference spaces, a theater, and multi-sport simulator rooms to name a few.

“Alba Palm Beach is introducing a limited-edition waterfront lifestyle that is hard to come by in today’s market, particularly in South Florida,” says Kenneth Baboun, developer of Alba Palm Beach. “We are creating a luxury destination where the best of high-caliber products, high-quality workmanship, and elevated amenities meet.”

Baboun assures future residents that Alba’s location on the west bank of the Intracoastal will offer residents access to the best of the best—enjoy exploring local beaches, shopping on Worth Avenue, boating on the Atlantic, checking out Palm Beach’s arts and culture scene, and more.

Architect Spina O’Rourke helms Alba Palm Beach’s interior design while Schmidt Nichols is behind the landscape/hardscape work to transform Alba’s 22-story tower, plus four two- and three-story townhomes. Set on 1.71 acres with direct Intracoastal frontage, Alba's waterfront condos are guaranteed to effortlessly evoke desirable coastal living vibes.
As of December, the development team says they received “a strong response” in pre-sales with more than 30 percent of the building reserved. View Alba Palm Beach's Waterfront Condos for Sale here.

LiveModern spoke with Mr. Kenneth Baboun, developer of Alba Palm Beach to learn more about this new Palm Beach development set to debut in 2025. Read along to discover why you should have your eye on this new gem.
What are the benefits of living in a boutique-sized property like Alba?

KB: The attention to detail in a boutique property is unmatched. The experience is really tailored to the specific resident at the development with unique touches like privacy and personalized conveniences.
Walk us through each residential unit.

KB: Each residence offers spacious layouts with in-demand amenities, including summer kitchens on the 16-foot-deep terraces, floor-to-ceiling hurricane impact windows, Italian-designed kitchens by Italkraft, spa-like baths, and smart-home technology. All the residences will be move-in ready.

SubZero, Wolf, and Cove's high-end appliances will be used with integrated paneling. All kitchens and bathrooms will be finished with matte lacquer cabinetry in two different color palettes and a quartz countertop with a reverse bevel knife edge. Bathrooms will find illuminated vanity mirrors. Primary bathrooms (in select residences) will include a decorative book-match accent porcelain slab wall. Grand outdoor terraces and summer kitchens will include SubZero, Wolf, and Electri-Chef grills.
Tell us about the designer finishes and materials in Alba’s common areas.

The common areas will incorporate an exquisite mix of stone and wood finishes throughout. The sixth-floor resident-only lounge and the wellness amenities include a sunset and sunrise pool, spa, gym, yoga, treatment room, and a private dining room with owners’ wine lockers.
Describe the technological features on display.

KB: Each residence will be equipped with smart-home technology, which will be able to support lighting, shades, sound, and thermostat. Each parking space will be EV-ready for those residents that have an electric vehicle.
What kinds of amenities can future guests expect to enjoy daily?

KB: There will be on-site resident conveniences, which will include pool towel service, 24/7 valet, and concierge services. The on-site private dock will provide our residents with an instant connection to the water for boating, sailing, kayaking, and paddle-boarding excursions. As a closing gift, Alba Palm Beach is offering each residence a coveted 1-year membership to the Palm Beach Yacht Club.
What else should we know about Alba’s Palm Beach debut?

KB: The Alba Palm Beach development team wanted to create the most sophisticated residential condominium on the market with exquisite materials and expansive layouts capturing the most dynamic views of Palm Beach and beyond. Pairing privacy and tranquility and exceptional access to nearby entertainment and world-class dining and shopping. Listening to the market and what buyers wanted in a primary residence or pied-a-terre, is what makes Alba Palm Beach so exceptional.

With a collection of 55 two- to three-bedroom residences, and four-bedroom townhomes with direct waterfront views, Alba is a can’t-miss opportunity for future living. Introductory pricing starts at $2.5 million. For more information on Alba Palm Beach waterfront condos or to discover what makes this the ultimate waterfront locale, contact us today.

title: Alina Residences Boca Raton: Behind the Design
h1: ALINA Residences Boca Raton
meta: Behind the Design: ALINA Residences Boca Raton

blocks.text: Jorge Garcia and Peter Stromberg (GS4 Studios) have created architecture and interior design solutions for hospitality, residential, and mixed-use buildings such as The Boca Raton, One Thousand Ocean at the Boca inlet, Pier 66, Adagio Residences, and Cypress City Center in Fort Lauderdale under their eponymous firm for over 30 years in South Florida. So it’s no surprise they were tapped to take the lead behind the design of ALINA Residences—downtown Boca Raton’s “most thoughtfully conceived new, private community.”

Developed by El-Ad National Properties, Alina Residences Boca Raton comprises a nine-story, 121-residence building featuring resort-inspired amenities focused on wellness. In March, El-Ad National broke ground on the second and final phase: two new residential buildings, ALINA 210 and ALINA 220, set to debut in 2024. The three towers will feature one to four bedrooms (ranging from $2 million - $8 million), many of which overlook The Boca Raton golf course.

Once completed, the luxury property will comprise 303 units plus a myriad of shared spaces and amenities across the expansive campus with a strong focus on wellness. Think: two private rooftop pools, outdoor dining and entertainment areas, two dog parks, his and hers Spas with dry saunas, steam rooms, treatment rooms, and indoor and outdoor relaxation rooms—plus plenty more!

Ready to learn more? Here, we spoke with Stromberg and Jackelin Rodriguez, director of interiors at Garcia Stromberg/GS4 Studios, to chat more about what went into the design of Boca’s ALINA Residences.
What made you both want to take on the design of Alina Residences?

Stromberg: ALINA Residences offered an opportunity to create a modern luxury residential project in the heart of Boca Raton. The site presented a tremendous opportunity to create custom residences that could simultaneously enjoy privacy and security, the convenience and benefits of living downtown, and the exclusivity and views offered by bordering The Boca Raton golf course and property. The site geometry and size allowed for the incorporation of tremendous outdoor living and gathering experiences. It is the ultimate Florida living.
Where did you draw inspiration for this project?

Stromberg: Studying the site's natural characteristics and geometries was the inspiration for the overall project layout, maximizing residential views and indoor/outdoor living environments. The building forms were designed to break down the overall scale of the project into a more boutique living experience and maximize the building perimeter to interact with the outdoor environment. The building materials selected are timeless in their attributes and are placed to complement the natural tones and hues of the surrounding landscape.
What can you tell us about the feel, color scheme, and vision of the units?

Rodriguez: Each unit is conceived as a unique custom residence with access from semi-private elevators or an interior gallery space. The concept is to open the resident up to natural light and views throughout the residence in as many areas as possible. The kitchens are inter-connected with the living spaces to allow for the best gathering and entertainment environment. The majority of residences open up to expansive outdoor living room spaces along the width of the unit.
The feel and finishes at ALINA Residences were tailored to accommodate and fit the taste and individual lifestyle of their exclusive buyers.

Color schemes are maintained within a neutral palette of soft muted light tones of whites and greys that enhance the feel of the open plan layouts, allowing each owner to customize their spaces through furnishing and art.

High-end porcelains and natural stones, in conjunction with lacquer cabinetry and walnut wood accents, fill the space for a harmonious palette. The plumbing fixtures' sleek lines and sheen, act as appointed jewelry through the kitchen and bathrooms.
Who did you work with to bring your design vision to life?

Stromberg: Our internal team of around 10-15 architects and interior designers, landscape architects, engineers, various consultants, attorneys, the City of Boca Raton, and of course, a client with a vision.
If you envision yourself as a future resident of ALINA Residences and its quaint, wellness-driven community, you can learn more about the property here.

title: Behind the Design: Four Seasons Hotel and Residences Fort Lauderdale
h1: Behind the Design: Four Seasons Hotel and Residences Fort Lauderdale
meta: Get a sneak peak into the design of the Four Seasons Hotel and Residences, Fort Lauderdale in this interview with British designer Tara Bernerd and her skilled design firm.

blocks.text: Four Seasons Hotel and Residences Fort Lauderdale recently made its grand debut along Fort Lauderdale Beach in March, joining the brand’s four existing properties in Florida. As one might expect, the hotel and its residences exude a lavish South Florida lifestyle that will beckon guests and future residents for years to come.

The 189-room hotel alone boasts a sophisticated style and energy to match the lively beachfront city and features several alluring points of interest from the lavish rooftop pool to the lobby lounge, Honey Fitz, to a sumptuous spa with can’t-miss spa water journey experiences, and of course, the crown culinary jewel: Evelyn’s. Named after the Fort Lauderdale luminary Evelyn Fortune Bartlett, the fine-dining concept blends coastal Florida cuisine with eastern Mediterranean.

The 83 private residences make up the other half of the Four Seasons’ latest gem. Aside from private gardens designed by landscape architect Fernando Wong, each living space is replete with marine-inspired details, creating a bright, modern, and airy escape.

Bringing these elements is British designer Tara Bernerd, whose firm oversaw the design of the guest rooms and public spaces. She worked alongside South Florida’s very own Kobi Karp, who’s behind the architecture to bring something truly magical to the city.

Ahead of your next visit to the property—or perhaps you’d like to call it home—we chatted with Bernerd and her team about all things design and to learn more about what sophisticated lifestyle awaits.
Where did you draw inspiration behind the design of Four Seasons Fort Lauderdale?

From my very first visit to Fort Lauderdale, I fell in love with the city, which has such a chic, low-key feeling of luxury to it. With the Four Seasons Fort Lauderdale, I really wanted to build on this and to allude back to its design heyday with the aesthetic of the space. We took inspiration from Fort Lauderdale’s yachting heritage, encapsulating the freshness and elegance of mid-century modernist design, an era of Chris Craft yachts and Capri pants; marrying a Riviera lifestyle with a luxury Florida/Bahamas feel, to create an approachable luxury with a timeless quality.

Our approach to design is always a layered process. We try to ensure that each project we undertake is indigenous to the surrounding environment, and we, therefore, find each one has its own unique identity. By drawing upon the local culture, history, climate, and atmosphere of a place, we seek to create a design DNA that completely embraces the character and identity of the place it is set. With that said, there is a thread that runs through our projects, from the considered layouts to the choice of finishes and bold use of layered materials.
What makes this property stand out from other Four Seasons?

I wanted to stay true to that Floridian style, pay homage to the Bahamas with some of the furniture, but there’s something about the Riviera—the Riva boats, the highly varnished woods. I wanted the hotel to have expressions of something like a hybrid that reminds me of the feeling of the Mediterranean and then pull in the threads of what I’ve been learning about Fort Lauderdale.
Walk us through the design of the residences—tell us about the general feel, color scheme, and vision.

As the residences are Four Seasons branded, our designs are clearly related to the main hotel, but the palette has been subtly morphed to suit the homes. Great attention has been paid to the layouts throughout the apartments to optimize the views and imbue a sense of drama. We bespoke designed the kitchens, utilizing minimalist lines and bold shapes that are complemented by a calming palette of natural materials, with high gloss white cupboards that set off the Arabascato countertops.The base of the kitchen island is finished in a warm café con leche wood, with antique brass accents that all tone beautifully with the travertine floors to add to the overall feeling of substance.
Tell us about the hotel’s aesthetic.

The design throughout the hotel and residences is a contemporary take on mid-century modernism that brings an elevated yet understated luxury. Signature materials include travertine, which has been used extensively throughout the hotel, from flooring to walls. High gloss walnut evokes the spirit of yachts and has been used throughout the lobbies and guest rooms, for furniture where it has also been teamed with rattan for headboards, chairs, and so on.

The main lobby was envisaged as an elegant oasis in the heart of the building. The boldly patterned floor has been inspired by Gio Ponti’s designs and is formed of triangular cuts of four contrasting coloured travertine and other stones in a striking geometric pattern. In the main part of the lobby, furniture groupings have been placed upon two large Tara Bernerd & Partners designed burgundy and cream rugs in bold blocky patterns. Mid-century inspired, free-standing bookcases help to divide and define the space into distinct areas.

The palette has been kept fresh and light with pale whites and pale grey linens, navy and white herringbone fabrics, navy leathers and the occasional pop of cognac ambers and burgundy. A Tara Bernerd & Partners’ bespoke-designed jute chandelier brings an added feeling of drama to the space, while elsewhere in the lobby mid-century inspired lights and lamps, together with books and objet d’art bring a pleasing feeling of home-away-from-home to the space.

The guest rooms continue themes explored in the lobby, with floors finished in a complimentary silver roman travertine. A circular bespoke Tara Bernerd & Partners striped rug in cream and navy blue brings a playful touch to the room and the walls have been papered with a natural pale linen effect wallpaper.
Who else did you work with to bring your vision to life?

Whilst this is our third Four Seasons to open, this project was actually our first appointment with the brand. I think relationships are terribly important and are a key part of any project, on top of the design skills and all the necessary countless other processes you have to take. It truly was a joy to work with ownership and the team at Four Seasons, whom I now consider to be friends as well as colleagues. Landscape designer Fernando Wong has designed sumptuous landscaping that surrounds the hotel, grounding it in the locale. Fellow British designer Martin Brudnizki has designed the main restaurant in the hotel, Evelyn’s.
What was it like working with South Florida–based architect Kobi Karp?

Kobi Karp and his team were a joy to work with, and we felt a great synergy, as they were also inspired by the vernacular of the environment in which the hotel is set. The hotel and residences are dual aspect, with one side facing the Atlantic Ocean and the other looking toward the Intracoastal Waterway. The architecture is echoed in the shape of the rooms and we worked particularly closely with Kobi Karp for the layouts throughout the property.
Conclusion

This is a must stay and must live property if you are looking for a home (or stay) with a view and understated, yet timeless luxury. Tara Bernerd & Partners were able to beautifully blend the life and spirit of Fort Lauderdale, FL with the stunning interiors of The Four Seasons. When asked what future resident would enjoy most about living at the Four Seasons Fort Lauderdale? She said “the dual aspect of the property looks towards the ocean and the sunrise on one side and the Intracoastal Waterway, with the stunning Floridian sunsets on the other.”
We’d love to give a special thanks to Tara Bernard and her team, as well as Lucien Smith Partner and Head of Communications for such a wonderful interview.

title: Best New Construction Condos In Brickell
h1: Best New Construction Condos In Brickell
meta: BEST NEW CONDOS TO LIVE IN BRICKELL. With so much New Construction popping up in Miami we wanted to help you with the choice of where to live by highlighting the best new condo buildings coming to bustling Brickell very shortly!

blocks.text: It seems everywhere you look these days, a new residential building is going up in South Florida. Miami is at the front of this development boom, with new condos being built in each of its neighborhoods. This trend began during the early days of the pandemic when people started flocking from high-tax states like New York and California in search of a place to live and work in an idyllic setting. In Miami alone, more than 52 new condos were announced last year in response to the recent wave of out-of-state residents.

One Miami neighborhood that’s quickly growing is Brickell. This area is known for its walkability, sky-high towers, scenic views, and location near sought-after spots such as Brickell City Centre and an array of drool-worthy restaurants.

So what are the best new construction condos to live in Brickell today? To start, we’ve compiled a list of new buildings in Miami’s Financial District that are on their way up, close to completion, or brand new. Whether you are a buyer, seller, or investor, these are the hotspot buildings in Brickell that you need to know now. Here’s what to expect if you’re planning to move.
Natiivo

This vertically integrated community stands 51 stories high and features 448 condo residences, a 240-unit hotel named Gale Miami, plus the private social club Natiivo Social with over 70,000 square feet of amenities and a culinary experience by LDV Hospitality. Prices start at $480,000.

In addition, future residents can enjoy a resort-style pool and fitness center inclusive of a Peleton studio, boxing ring, yoga and pilates lounge, and more. It’s also home to Creative HQ, Miami’s first-ever creative office space. Hospitality options abound with a street-front restaurant featuring an outdoor terrace and a spin-off concept of Miami’s famed “Bodega Taqueria y Tequila”.

The development recently announced a partnership with Baia Beach Club, offering Natiivo buyers and brokers access to the beach club inside Mondrian South Beach during construction. Buyers will have membership access to the club for one year after the development opens.
Baccarat Residences Brickell
444 Brickell Ave. Suite 800, Miami, FL 33131

Dreamt up by Related Group in collaboration with SH Hotels and Resorts is Baccarat Residences Brickell. The new 75-story tower offers 360 luxury units, including tower residences, penthouses, and riverfront villas, starting from $1.4 million. Its location showcases views of Biscayne Bay, the Miami River, and the city skyline. This marks Baccarat’s first collection of residences in the Miami market.

Arquitectonica designed the project to resemble waves in a sea of glass, inspired by Baccarat’s iconic crystal creations. According to its website, the interiors blend 18th-century French design with 1930s Art Deco to create a “uniquely Miami feel,” including handcrafted Baccarat crystals at every turn.

The tower’s five-star amenities include a fitness/wellness center inclusive of a Baccarat Hammam spa with steam room, meditation rooms, a club room featuring billiards, a wine cellar and a tasting room, plus private dining with a chef’s kitchen. Amenities include multiple pools, private cabanas, a private water taxi service, and Beach Club membership at 1 Hotel South Beach.
Lofty Brickell

Another Brickell residence on its way up is Lofty, designed with the nomad lifestyle in mind. These residences will feature turn-key, fully furnished residences designed for those who want to spend more time working remotely. Located on the Miami River, the building will house studios, one- and two-bedroom apartments, and penthouses ranging from $550K to $3.3 million.

The flexible-ownership condo partnered with global furnishing brand Artefacto with a collection of branded penthouses dubbed Lofty PH Collection, which will occupy the tower’s top nine floors. Artefacto x INC—a multi-disciplinary New York studio—is behind the design. Future owners can utilize a private marina, invitation-only speakeasy, rooftop deck with infinity pool, co-working spaces, juice bar, and social club.

Lofty also launched the first-ever NFT art collective designed for condo owners. Each resident will receive a custom NFT upon a purchase of their residence, which marks a first for a condominium to commission and display an NFT collection. The artwork is done by four renowned artists in collaboration with Miami-based artist Carlos Betancourt, whose works focus on issues such as culture, memory, and beauty.
Cipriani Residences Miami

The iconic Cipriani family, known for operating the famed Harry’s Pizza in Venice, Italy, has since expanded over the decades into an international hospitality empire featuring restaurants, luxury residential properties, private membership clubs, and more. Their latest venture is the Cipriani Residences in collaboration with the design firm 1508 London, which “captures the spirit of the brand.”

There are 397 residences, ranging from one to four bedrooms, which start at $1.1 million. Inside, rooms house 10-foot ceilings, floor-to-glass windows, expansive terraces, plus panoramic views of sights like Biscayne Bay and the downtown skyline. Eat-in chef kitchens feature Italian cabinetry, Wolf Sub-Zero appliances, and Art Deco–inspired lighting. The bathrooms include a glass-enclosed shower, stand-alone bathtub, Dornbracht fixtures, and a back-lit mirror.

Future residents can enjoy around-the-clock dining services by Cipriani (either in-home or private, reservable dining rooms). Other amenities include a sun terrace, two swimming pools outfitted with loungers and cabanas, a state-of-the-art fitness center, private screening room, holistic spa and sauna, pickleball court, golf simulator, and more.
MAJOR Residences

The latest residential building set to debut in Brickell is MAJOR Residences, which will stand at 1,049 feet upon completion and feature 259 one- to four-bedroom hybrid condo-hotel units. Prices start at $1.6 million.

The residences are the brainchild of Major Food Group (MFG) co-founders Mario Carbone, Jeff Zalaznick, and Rich Torrisi, who are behind some of America’s most sought-after restaurants and hospitality venues, including Miami-based Carbone, Dirty French, and ZZ’s Club. The founders partnered with developer and builder JDS Development Group to create the sophisticated project, which marks a milestone for MFG in its global portfolio.

To no surprise, the residences will offer an impressive collection of food and beverage amenities. Famed design firms Studio Sofield and Ken Fulk Inc. will oversee the complete design. Future residents can access a silver and gold swimming pool, plus a handful of other luxe amenities, which will be announced closer to the new year.

title: Best Rooftop Bars in Fort Lauderdale
h1: Top Fort Lauderdale Rooftop Bars
meta: Best Rooftop Bars in Fort Lauderdale

blocks.text: You can discover what makes Fort Lauderdale a great city on foot, but things get even better when you’re up in the air. We’re talking rooftop bars, and the city’s recent boom in alfresco dining and entertaining makes imbibing with friends even more enticing. Good news for locals—you can enjoy this experience year-round in South Florida, courtesy of the pleasant climate.

Whether you’re looking for a date-night spot or want a change of scenery for happy hour, we’ve rounded up the best rooftop bars in Fort Lauderdale sure to have you coming back for more. Even better? There are plenty to choose from in various neighborhoods, so you’ll never have a shortage of epic views or destinations to enjoy. Cheers to that!
Rooftop @1WLO

First on the list is what set the scene for a rooftop boom in Fort Lauderdale: Rooftop @1WLO. Nestled atop TRP Taste on Las Olas Boulevard, this garden-inspired hangout offers panoramic views of the skyline plus a chill yet sophisticated vibe. The hand-crafted cocktails steal the show, so be sure to order the OG Garden Party cocktail or Into the Sunset. Happy hour runs Wednesday-Friday from 4 p.m. to 6 p.m. and Saturdays from 2 p.m. to 4 p.m. Enjoy $7 select spirits, $10 cocktails, and nibbles for $8. For those looking to get the weekend started early, head over for brunch with food offerings ranging from Straw-Guava French Toast to Bulgogi Beef Skewers.
The lowdown: Located in the heart of downtown, Rooftop @1WLO is within walking distance to residences such as 100 Las Olas and the Main Las Olas, making it a go-to neighborhood spot.
Sparrow

Perched 25 floors above ground, Sparrow is the tallest rooftop to grace Fort Lauderdale. It’s located inside the posh Dalmar Hotel on Federal Highway downtown and is a no-brainer to include on our rooftop bar list. From vibe-heavy DJ’s playing weekly to a food menu that’s just as impressive as its bar program, this hangout has all the makings of an excellent night out. If you go, order the Passion No. 5 cocktail and lobster mac n cheese, though you can never choose wrong here. Happy hour runs from 5 p.m. to 7 p.m. during the week.
The lowdown: The Dalmar sits on the edge of Flagler Village and is accessible by locals in downtown proper. It's also a quick uber ride away for those closer to the beach.
The Easton Rooftop Pool & Lounge

This newcomer arrived on the scene last year with a debut featuring performer Diplo and a packed house. Don’t be fooled—while it sits atop The Courtyard Marriott in Flagler Village, this rooftop is decked out with a 2,000-square-foot pool deck and has the makings of a great time. Locals stop by for happy hour to soak in some city views and enjoy fun tipples like the Let’s Flamingle Punch, ideal for small groups. There’s also some great food selections including pool bites and heartier options for those who work up an appetite.
The lowdown: Also located in Fort Lauderdale’s Flagler Village neighborhood, the Easton is a quick Uber or walk away for young professionals.
BarRita

This Fort Lauderdale rooftop might be located only one floor above ground, but BarRita is still a must on this list. What’s not to love about noshing on chips, queso, and delicious margaritas with a view of the city in the distance? This outpost garners a crowd every day of the week—not just Taco Tuesdays—and is known for its colorful facade, glowing roof, and high-energy vibe. Be sure to make a reservation ahead of time as spaces fill up fast, especially during happy hour from 3:30 p.m. to 6:30 p.m. Monday-Friday. Order from a fun cocktail list including favorites like the Pineapple Express or the Don’t Be a Prick. Stay for the truffle street corn guac and diverse tacos ranging from Baja grilled shrimp to Wagyu beef birria. There’s also an epic “Buenos Dias” brunch menu offering everyone’s favorites mixed in with a few sweet and savory picks like the Tres Leches French Toast.

The lowdown: Set just south of downtown, this bar is nestled in Poinciana Park on Andrews Avenue, making it an easy drive for those who live at downtown hotspots such as the Icon Las Olas, 100 Las Olas, and more.
McSorley’s Beach Pub

Headed to the beach? One rooftop bar worth checking out after a day of surf and sand is McSorley’s. A staple among locals who have called Laudy home over the decades, this Ireland-inspired pub and bar is set among three floors with a different vibe offered at each. Make your way to the third-floor terrace for beautiful expanses of the ocean and cool off with a drink in hand. This is a laidback spot for happy hour or to catch the big game on the big screens.
The lowdown: This rooftop is accessible by a handful of beachfront properties including the new Four Seasons Resort & Residences, Paramount Fort Lauderdale, W Residences, and more.
Live and Play in Fort Lauderdale

If you need other great ideas from a local, LiveModern would love to connect and help immerse you in all things Fort Lauderdale. We specialize in matching homebuyers with real estate based on their lifestyle, whether that includes boating, beachfront living, waterfront or downtown properties near entertainment, shopping and more.

title: Best Waterfront Restaurants near Boca Raton
h1: Best Waterfront Restaurants Near Boca Raton
meta: The most exclusive dining experiences have an exceptional atmosphere and a sensational view! Check out the best waterfront dining near Boca Raton here!

blocks.text: Sprawling white sand beaches, clear azure waves gently lapping the shore, and soft breezes that lightly caress your face to wake you from slumber. This is life in heaven, known by locals as Boca Raton.

While Boca Raton may not be a city that readily comes to mind when you think of luxury destinations across the globe, it is a best kept secret known in South Florida known for its prestige, country clubs and luxury lifestyle. This city boasts fantastic outdoor activities, a world-class art museum, and exceptional dining experiences to satisfy any palette.

At LiveModern, our clients expect only the best from their homes and vacation properties, including the cities those properties are in. Our goal is always to grant you a level of exclusivity and splendor you can’t find anywhere else.

That’s why we’ve documented what we think are the absolute best restaurants on the water near Boca Raton FL. This Palm Beach County city is extremely sought after, dubbed "the Beverly Hills of Florida". Any place you spend significant amounts of time will need to offer significant amenities. Remember, you’re not just buying a property; you’re investing into your new lifestyle and that includes location.
Trust us when we say you’ll be spoilt for choice with the fine dining!
The Best Waterfront Restaurants near Boca Raton

Qualifying for our list of the best restaurants on the water near Boca Raton is no easy feat. Those on the list need to have exceptional cuisine, unparalleled service, and of course, stunning vistas. There’s quite simply nothing more satisfying than delectable food and nourishing company as the sun delicately paints the water and sky orange.
1. Chanson at the Royal Blues Hotel

Opulence comes to mind when we think of the Chanson at the Royal Blues Hotel. Every culinary desire and wish is catered to taking you on a seasonal journey of scrumptious South Florida. Their menu often changes, meaning just one visit isn’t enough to sample all they have to offer.

We put the Chanson up there with the best ocean view restaurants near Boca Raton for its expansive sea views that are sure to impress. Serving breakfast, lunch, brunch, and dinner, everyone is sure to find something they love. Make sure you book in advance and request outdoor seating at sunset for the best spot to watch the show!
2. Latitudes

Latitudes at the Delray Sands Resort is one of those culinary institutions that only comes around once in a hundred years. With five different dining spaces and a modern decor clearly influenced by the wavering blue hues of the ocean, Latitudes has earned its place as one of our top picks for ocean view restaurants.

Featuring a distinctly South Florida vibe and views of the endless ocean in all directions, it’s the perfect spot to bring a significant other for cocktails and seafood while the sun dips below the waves.
3. The Cove Waterfront Restaurant and Tiki Bar
A great dining experience is not the only thing on the menu at The Cove Waterfront Restaurant. For waterfront dining near Boca Raton, the views just can’t be beaten.

With a menu ranging from the freshest seafood on the east coast to comfort food favorites such as burgers and craft beers, The Cove is without a doubt one of the best restaurants to travel to via boat, park, and eat on the water.

Gorgeous views of the Intracoastal make it the perfect setting for meeting friends for drinks at the tiki bar to catch up while enjoying the view. Imagine parking your yacht after a day on the water and strolling over as they greet you with a gin and tonic at sunset.
4. Oceans 234
For the best fine dining near Boca Raton, Oceans 234 tops the list. World-renowned seafood dishes and awe-inspiring views make this an excellent choice for a special meal with that special someone.

For dinner, we recommend the Thai pesto Mahi and shrimp or the Ora king salmon for unique and mouth-watering takes on scrumptious seafood. Why not start the journey with one of their signature cocktails like Mango on Fire or Seaside Sangria to match the rays of sun dancing on the waves?

In addition, Oceans 234 will be offering the talents of top-tier chefs throughout 2022, making a dining experience here not to be missed. Imagine savoring the skill, love, and craftsmanship in every bite of the dishes prepared by experts such as their current chef-in-residence, Chef Paula, a Hell’s Kitchen veteran and master of magnificent flavors.
5. JB’s On the Beach

If you’re looking for that laid-back beach vibe, JB’s On The Beach is where you need to be. With its succulent seafood and outdoor seating only a few steps away from the ocean, this makes JB’s one of the best waterfront restaurants near Boca Raton.
Sip on one of their signature cocktails while you watch the world go by from the comfort of JB’s terrace. There’s simply no better way to spend an evening with friends or loved ones.
Start Your New Waterfront Life in Boca Raton

Stunning natural beauty and year-round summer aren’t the only luxuries in this city. At LiveModern, we know that experiences are what this life is really all about, and waterfront dining offers a feast for the senses that is second to none.
But these delicious delicacies don’t have to be a one-off meal. If you’re considering a move to Boca Raton, get in touch with one of our agents for a consultation today.

We focus only on the best real estate in South Florida, so you can rest assured each one is hand-picked by our experts with specialized knowledge of the area and local markets. So let us help you live well and LiveModern!
