title: Brightline Connects Orlando and South Florida
h1: Brightline Connects Orlando & South Florida
meta: Picture this: zipping from West Palm Beach's vibrant coast to Orlando’s world-famous theme parks in record time, leaving traffic headaches far behind. While this idea may have seemed like a dream just a few years ago, it’s now within reach thanks to

blocks.text: Picture this: zipping from West Palm Beach's vibrant coast to Orlando’s world-famous theme parks in record time, leaving traffic headaches far behind. While this idea may have seemed like a dream just a few years ago, it’s now within reach thanks to the monumental completion of Brightline's Orlando station located at Orlando International Airport.
Brightline estimates 35 million people travel between Central Florida and Miami each year.

With its sleek and cutting-edge high-speed rail, the company is poised to revolutionize Florida's transportation landscape, seamlessly connecting the heart of the Sunshine State to its bustling southern hubs.

According to Brightline's media relations director, Vanessa Afonso, today's travelers crave more than just an affordable ticket; they desire an enjoyable experience. “We offer a guest-first experience, and we believe it will push others in our industry to do the same,” she says. “Each train and station is meticulously designed and built through the lens of today’s modern traveler.”

Tickets for rides beginning in September are available for purchase with locals and visitors alike eager to hop on board and explore. Brightline plans to operate 16 round trips between Orlando and Miami, with one-way fares starting at $79 per trip. Brightline service between Central and South Florida will depart southbound from Orlando Station and northbound from MiamiCentral on an hourly schedule. The station will still offer its usual commuter routes for South Floridians with stations in West Palm Beach, Boca Raton, Fort Lauderdale, Aventura, and Miami.
The best part? Brightline is designed to connect and partner with local and public transportation networks to improve connectivity, so traveling to nearby cities is always a breeze.
Ready to learn more about Brightline’s game-changing new station and what this means for South Florida? We spoke with Afonso to learn all about what to expect.
LM: Can you tell us about the infrastructure improvements and investments made to accommodate the expansion into Orlando?

VA: Construction teams worked over seven million hours battling the COVID-19 pandemic and supply chain shortages to finish the 170-mile corridor extension. Brightline’s Orlando extension project includes a state-of-the-art vehicle maintenance facility, a buildout of the Orlando station at Orlando International Airport, 56 bridges, three underpasses, and upgrades to 156 railroad crossings. Completion of engineering achievements includes over two million spikes and bolts, 2.3 million tons of granite and limestone were transported by 20,000 railcars, 225 million pounds of 100 percent recycled American steel, 6 million cubic yards of rail embankment fill, and 450,000 concrete ties.
What can passengers expect in terms of travel experience and amenities when using Brightline trains to travel to and from Orlando?
Guests can anticipate an experience at the intersection of transportation and hospitality.

Food and beverage service onboard (complimentary in PREMIUM), Starlink Wifi, leather stitched seats, and plenty of charging ports. All trains feature enhanced accessibility and are fully ADA-accessible.
What efforts has Brightline made to ensure environmental sustainability and reduce its carbon footprint with this expansion?

Brightline is the most environmentally friendly biodiesel-electric train in service. Its locomotives meet the highest emission standards set by the federal government. Riding the train reduces CO2 emissions by 75 percent per passenger mile (or kilometer) compared to traveling by a personal vehicle. This service will remove 3 million cars per year from area roadways!
How do you foresee the expansion into Orlando contributing to economic growth and development in the region?

The investment made by Brightline has spurred significant commercial and residential growth as proof that transit-oriented development works and more is needed. Transit stations bring opportunities for cities looking for ways to bring more affordable housing and other projects that generate jobs and options for commuters. Brightline’s stations have led to thousands of new units — including affordable housing — and have regenerated areas of Miami and South Florida that hadn’t been redeveloped in decades.

title: Defining Luxury with The Estates at Acqualina
h1: Defining Luxury with The Estates at Acqualina
meta: Behind the design of The Estates at Acqualina

blocks.text: Tucked away on Sunny Isles Beach lies Acqualina Resort & Residences, one of the U.S.’s most iconic properties, which has been recognized for a fourth consecutive year as one of the best resorts in the U.S. by U.S. News & World Report. Exceptional guest service, pristine grounds, and luxury accommodations are a few reasons it earned this designation.

It’s no surprise the resort’s sister property, The Estates at Acqualina, is turning heads for its own accolades—and is one of the fastest properties to sell in South Florida. Developed by Eddie Trump, Jules Trump, and Stephanie Trump, The Estates feature two 50-story towers. The South Tower, topping off at 646 feet, was completed this year, and the 91-residence North Tower is set to finish in 2023.

As the newest building on Acqualina grounds, the $1.8 billion residential development is a spectacle. Residences can enjoy a range of wow factors from furnished penthouses, exclusive indoor and outdoor amenities, lobby designs by the late fashion icon Karl Lagerfeld, and five-star services from the luxury resort, including Avra Miami, a new 12,000-square-foot Greek restaurant by Nick Tsoulos, Nick Pashalis, and Marc Packer.

Bringing a luxe feel to The Estates is the husband-and-wife team Todd and Isabel Tragash of STA Architectural Group, which was established in 1985 in Miami Beach. Since its inception, the couple and its team of 10 have produced a diverse breadth of work including residential, commercial, and hospitality projects.

“We enjoy working closely with our clients on projects where thoughtful planning is required, and materiality, art, and design are driving forces,” says Isabel. “Our projects span from architecture to interior design. We like to say we do not specialize in a project type, but we select our clients carefully.”

They are part of the Mila Miami restaurant team, providing architectural services for their talented designers. Todd and Isabel also designed a new building for The Generator Hotel in Miami Beach, which is expected to break ground this summer. Most recently, they completed a number of The Estates’ private units in the South Tower and are close to wrapping up more in the North Tower. Below, Todd and Isabel share more about the vision behind The Estates at Acqualina.
What excited you about working with The Estates at Acqualina?

IT: We began working with the Trump Group 18 years ago when they selected us to design ESPA at the Acqualina Resort & Spa. It was both an opportunity and a welcomed challenge to design their five-star spa and wellness center. It was our introduction to their newly branded luxury lifestyle. When they launched their next project, Mansions at Acqualina, our role expanded to design the residential units and all of the common areas. They always strived to raise the bar of the Acqualina brand and challenged us to do the same at The Estates.
Tell us about the design inspiration behind the residences.

IT: Our inspiration drew from the relaxed beachfront setting and the lifestyle of effortless elegance residents seek when they choose to live or vacation in Miami. We embrace open-plan layouts, which enhance the views and allow natural light to pour into the living spaces. We match the seamless flow through spaces with carefully planned view alignments in each unit’s layout. Our finish materials are selected by creating moments of strong punctuations with backdrops of quiet materials. The notion of stunning views from every space, and pristine natural light punctuated with bold materials capture the essence of a modern Miami.
Walk us through the spaces you designed for The Estates.

TT: Over the years, we have developed a great relationship with the Trump Group and its remarkable architectural/engineering team. Our work started with the layout of the residential units. From there, we designed the various unit types. We developed the finished material selections, along with the designs of the kitchens, bathrooms, and specialty features. We designed the sales center that includes a full-scale model unit onsite with oceanfront views. Our role in the Villa common areas included the design of Avra, the banquette facilities, and AcquaFit.
What makes The Estates at Acqualina stand out from other South Florida properties?

IT: It is a combination of many things. Aside from the spectacular oceanfront site, the grand scale, and layout of each unit, you can find quality and attention to detail throughout. The novel element that stands out is the process where the buyers personalize their units by selecting from curated schemes of finishes, millwork, fixtures, and appliances.

We meet with every buyer to assist them in their selections. Our material schemes were developed and inspired by our travels to quarries around the world where we were able to see stones never seen before. During these trips, we gathered a trove of samples from which we develop two color palettes with an array of interchangeable stone options. The concept of highlighting certain features with a bolder stone remained a constant. For our softer materials, we brought in textured woods from white brushed white oak to cerused greige. By meeting with each resident and walking through the options, we were able to facilitate their selection process of having so many choices. Once the selection process was completed, each resident made their unit distinctly their own.
How did you incorporate technology into the design?

TT: Our approach to technology is to incorporate it as an invisible feature. Its purpose is to enhance the quality of living. Not to complicate your life. Every unit has its individual building system’s network to manage lighting, a/c, shades, audio/visual, etc. Our kitchen and bath designs incorporate the latest technology in millwork, fixtures, and appliances. Even our closet design incorporates sophisticated hardware and accessories to maximize storage capacity and illuminate display items elegantly.
You designed the highly anticipated Greek restaurant Avra. Tell us about this space.

IT: Avra is located on the main level of the villa, centrally situated between the North and South towers. It has a classical double-height front portico and rear terrace facing the ocean. It’s all open [floor] plan, with a bougainvillea-covered pergola built with kiln-dried, solid oak columns, beams and rafters, and natural twig lattice. The main dining room is 100 feet wide, with 25-foot ceilings facing the royal blue infinity pool, which appears to empty itself into the ocean. We selected Pentelikon marble quarried in Greece for the bar and exhibition kitchen countertops and for the floors, wide oak planks in a herringbone pattern with a travertine border. As a signature feature in all of Avra’s restaurants, we incorporated featured stonework; a tripartite salt stone sculpture under the pergola, and a three-dimensional composition of the extracted remnants of quarried blocks after they’re cut into slabs at each end of the dining room.
We’re also curious about Acquafit. What can residents look forward to here?

TT: Acquafit occupies the full floor on the top level of the villa. It combines both fitness and spa elements. In contrast to the villa’s linear composition, the entry design feature is a three-dimensional orb with a curvilinear skeletal frame. Scooped out of the front of the orb are the reception and juice bar. On the other end are the salt sauna and the spa treatment room. Acquafit is divided into three parallel segments all facing the ocean. In the center is the cardio fitness area. Flanking one side is the studio set up for spinning, TRX, yoga, and Pilates classes. Flanking the other side are the cardio, boxing ring, and stretching areas. The color palette is a striking contrast of black and white with splashes of “Dreaming Green” marble and etched jade mirror accents. Inset in the ceiling are mirror-polished stainless steel ovals that evoke a water ripple effect.
Ready to call The Estates at Acqualina home? Select residences are available. Check out Live Modern for more info on securing your new abode today.

title: Explore South Florida's 7 Most Cutting-Edge Buildings
h1: Explore South Florida's 7 Most Cutting-Edge Buildings
meta:
Top 7 Modern Buildings in South Florida Based on Architecture & Technology


In Miami, Broward, and Palm Beach counties, it's not just the weather that's heating...

blocks.text: In Miami, Broward, and Palm Beach counties, it's not just the weather that's heating up—the real estate market is on fire, too. Over the past few years, South Florida has experienced a real estate boom, bringing a slew of cool and modern buildings to the entire region. From sleek, high-tech skyscrapers that touch the clouds to eco-conscious wonders that embrace sustainability, South Florida's sprawling urban canvas has undergone a transformative evolution. Here, we’ve rounded up seven of the Tri-County's most modern buildings—both finished and under construction—that are turning heads for their architectural wonder and advanced technology.
WEST PALM BEACH
ALBA Palm Beach

BGI Companies—along with co-developer Blue Road Group are on a mission to redefine waterfront living in West Palm Beach with its newest development: Alba. Once complete, the 55 carefully designed, intimately sized residences will represent modern elegance in The Palm Beaches. Alba stands out from other properties because of its incredible amenities on the sixth floor, like a state-of-the-art theater and multi-sport simulator rooms. That’s not all—each residence offers spacious layouts featuring summer kitchens, spa-like baths, floor-to-ceiling hurricane impact windows, and Italian-designed kitchens by Italkraft. In addition, every residence comes furnished with cutting-edge smart-home technology, enabling residents to effortlessly control lighting, window shades, sound systems, and thermostat settings. Residents with electric vehicles will each get a parking space equipped and ready for EV charging.

West Palm Beach’s Spina O'Rourke + Partners designed the modern, sleek development, which features an elevated amenity deck with uninterrupted views of breathtaking sunrises, sunsets, and sparkling waters. Privacy is also a top priority, with only a limited number of residences on each floor. Private elevators add an extra layer of seclusion and exclusivity to this exceptional living experience.
The Bristol Palm Beach

With its gorgeous glass façade setting the scene, The Bristol Palm Beach blends timelessness with elegance inside the 25-story waterfront condominium. We love this property for its unobstructed views of the Intracoastal, Atlantic Ocean, and the island of Palm Beach. Once completed, The Bristol will offer three- to five-bedroom luxury homes ranging from 3,700 to 14,000 square feet, plus private guest suites.

Each residence is accessible through private elevators that open directly into the unit; separate service elevators are available for staff and deliveries. These residences are fully move-in ready and feature the highest-quality finishes, top-notch appliances, cutting-edge technologies, and all the modern conveniences one could desire. Living rooms can be adorned with white marble, limestone, or European white oak flooring; kitchens come with Gaggenau double ovens, gas and electric induction cooktops, double dishwashers, three Sub-Zero under-counter refrigerator drawers and 100-bottle wine cooler.

The best part? The amenities are said to rival those of 5-star resorts. Imagine a cutting-edge fitness center, a 75-foot lap pool for leisurely swims, versatile spaces for socializing and conducting business, an intimate private dining room offering mesmerizing Intracoastal vistas, lush garden terrace areas, and both wet and dry spa facilities, creating the perfect environment for relaxation and rejuvenation.
FORT LAUDERDALE
Four Seasons Hotel & Residences Fort Lauderdale

Reviving the vibrant spirit of Fort Lauderdale beach's waterfront is the Four Seasons Hotel and Residences Fort Lauderdale. This property features an 189-room hotel alongside 83 private residences, offering luxurious accommodations and impeccable services.

The property features a treasure trove of exceptional amenities that cater to everyone. A luxurious rooftop pool, a state-of-the-art fitness center, and a rejuvenating spa featuring captivating spa water journey experiences are just the beginning. Beyond these amenities are an inviting lobby lounge (Honey Fitz) and an exquisite on-site restaurant dubbed Evelyn's.

At The Four Seasons Fort Lauderdale, what’s on the inside is just as important as the outside. The property enlisted the expertise of British designer Tara Bernerd and South Florida's Kobi Karp to create something truly exceptional for the city. Bernerd's firm was responsible for the guest rooms and public space design, infusing them with her distinct style and sophistication. Collaborating with her was Kobi Karp, the acclaimed architect known for his work in the region. Together, they have brought a touch of magic to the city, ensuring that the property's design and architecture are nothing short of extraordinary.

The hotel and residences at Four Seasons Fort Lauderdale offer dual aspects, with one side providing breathtaking views of the Atlantic Ocean; the other side overlooks the scenic Intracoastal. The unique architecture of the building is reflected in the layout and design of the rooms. The residences come with private gardens designed by landscape architect Fernando Wong, each living space is replete with marine-inspired details, creating a bright, modern, and airy escape.
MIAMI
One Thousand Museum

One Thousand Museum is renowned for its futuristic and avant-garde design, which was the last residential building designed by the renowned architect Zaha Hadid before her passing. The building is a true testament to her innovative architectural vision. The 700-foot ultra luxury tower features 83 luxury condos (a mix of duplex townhouses, full-floor residences, and half-floor residences) and is most known first for one of its striking features—the exoskeleton, a structural system of curvilinear supports that not only provide stability but also serve as a distinctive architectural statement. This exoskeleton sets it apart from traditional skyscrapers and is a marvel of engineering and design. It also was built ahead of its time and was said to be designed to be “11% more efficient than current Florida Energy Code.”

Aside from the engineering marvels, the residences boast several distinctive features that set them apart. Notably, the lobby's ceiling stands out as a striking design element, formed by a series of rounded-edge panels that echo the same design motif seen at the reception desk. Additionally, the 60th-floor indoor pool presents a remarkable sight, featuring a waterfall shear wall adorned with a distinctive feathered pattern, adding an extra layer of elegance and artistry to the building's interior spaces.
Paramount Miami Worldcenter

Another jaw-dropping area downtown is Miami Worldcenter, a 27-acre mixed-use development. The $6 billion project includes residential, retail, hospitality, and commercial uses, with some projects open and completed, some are being built now, and some are planned for future phases. One of the most well-known projects is the Paramount Miami Worldcenter, built in 2019. The 569-unit signature luxury condominium tower is fully sold out to buyers from 60 countries around the world.

Perhaps one of the most captivating highlights is the property's awe-inspiring LED screen. It's renowned as the world's most technologically advanced Color Kinetics Lighting System, composed of an astonishing 13,400 light-emitting diodes (LEDs) artfully embedded in 10,000 panes of high-impact-resistant glass. This unique and visually stunning lighting system, which involved the efforts of 12 technicians over three years, is said to have come at a price tag of $3 million. It allows for the creation of a mesmerizing array of multi-million color combinations, ensuring that the experience of Paramount Miami Worldcenter residents is nothing short of extraordinary.
Aston Martin Residences

This residential tower is the result of a collaboration between the renowned British luxury car manufacturer Aston Martin and G&G Business Developments that’s giving new meaning to the car’s tagline: “Passion for Perfection.” It's not just a building with the Aston Martin name; the design and attention to detail reflect the brand's commitment to excellence and luxury.

Every living space within the Aston Martin Residences has been thoughtfully curated, offering a lavish array of features that cater to the highest standards of luxury. These features include top-of-the-line appliances, cutting-edge home technology, private terraces, and expansive floor-to-ceiling windows. Homeowners here experience a lifestyle of exclusivity, with access to four levels of Sky Amenities. These include a curated art gallery, a business center, conference rooms for productive meetings, wellness amenities for a balanced life, state-of-the-art movie theaters for entertainment, virtual golf for recreation, an upper-level fitness center for maintaining a healthy lifestyle, and an infinity-edge pool that provides breathtaking views of Miami's magnificent skyline.

The kitchens in Aston Martin Residences are a pinnacle of luxury, boasting Bulthaup cabinetry and Gaggenau appliances. The premium marble flooring adds an extra touch of opulence, setting the stage for a comfortable and elegantly designed living space.
Missoni Baia

Missoni Baia is ideally situated in Edgewater and offers residents breathtaking views of Biscayne Bay, the Atlantic Ocean, and the Miami skyline. The 57-story luxury condominium marks the Italian fashion house's first-ever branded residential tower. Missoni Baia’s modern architectural design and sleek, minimalist art-inspired façade was brought to life by architect Hani Rashid of Asymptote Architecture, with a richly layered landscape designed by Enzo Enea of Enea Garden Design.

The development is known for its unique and eye-catching architectural design. The signature feature is its distinctive "zig-zag" pattern on the facade. In this venture, Missoni's distinctive and lively design sensibility, featuring their trademark use of colors, finds expression not only in the tower's interiors but also in all communal spaces and amenities. Spearheaded by New York designer Paris Forino and creatively directed by Angela and Rosita Missoni themselves, the residences, furnishings, and artworks within the tower are elegantly crafted.

These thoughtfully designed homes feature 10-foot-high ceilings, spacious terraces measuring 8- to 10 feet deep, impact-resistant glass windows and sliding doors, floor-to-ceiling glazing, elegant bathrooms with European-style lacquered cabinets and mosaic tile floors, and custom-designed kitchen cabinetry with top-of-the-line appliances. Residents can enjoy five distinct pools, private cabanas, elevated tennis courts, an expansive spa with sauna and steam facilities, a state-of-the-art gym, dedicated yoga and Pilates studios, a private screening room, and more. Exclusive membership access to the Grand Bay Club, located on the beachfront of Miami's Key Biscayne, further enhances the lifestyle for residents.

South Florida has been reshaped by a real estate renaissance, ushering in a new era of modern and avant-garde architectural wonders. If you’re interested in learning more about these properties or would like to call one a home in the near future, you can check out all of the available listings at LiveModern.

title: Nautilus 220: The New Construction Craze
h1: Nautilus 220: The New Construction Craze
meta: A little insider knowledge on what's happening with the Nautilus 220 New Construction project right now, and why people are not only choosing new construction over resales but also still choosing Nautilus as their new home to be.

blocks.text: Yes, it's one of the most sought-after new construction condo projects in West Palm Beach. Yes, the features and amenities are simply top-notch. Yes, this building is a boater's dream and is bringing luxury to life. Hearing all of these great things get a little repetitive after a while, right?... Probably not! Because the building is just that great. But, what we DO want to do is give you a little insider knowledge on what's happening with the project right now, and why people are not only choosing new construction over resales but also still choosing Nautilus as their new home to be.

If you’ve thought about buying a new construction condo in West Palm Beach but somehow Nautilus hasn't been on your radar, you may want to reconsider especially before it’s too late. This building is one of the most attractive yet luxury pre-construction buildings, so much so that some of its floor plans are already completely sold out. It’s located in Lake Park, in the heart of The Palm Beaches, in close proximity to everything, including Palm Beach International Airport, the pristine beaches, world-class golf, and unrivaled dining and shopping. Not that you’d need, much less want to leave the property with the extensive list of on-site features and amenities.

Outside of the resort-style amenities, perfect location, and breathtakingly ambient backdrop, many people find Nautilus 220 to be very attractive because of its wide price point range. Nautilus condos initially started as low as $600k to around 3M depending on the floor plan (which is very uncommon for luxury new construction condos to be offered at such affordable prices). An additional perk for residents owning a boat or boating enthusiasts is the building partnered with the Marina so that owners have first availability to rent a dock right outside the building making aquatic adventures easy and convenient.

With Nautilus over 50% sold, units are still going fast and interest in the building is continuing to rise. The condos are now ranging in price from $800k to 3M+. Our preferred agents at The Modern Living Group were not only knowledgeable about this new construction project early in the pre-construction process but they were able to advocate for our clients, move quickly, and find them the perfect units to suit their needs before availability began to dwindle. Modern Living Group has sold 7 condos at Nautilus ranging from $700k to 1.64M and continues to work with clients who are interested in the building. Many clients who have the luxury of time and don't have an immediate need to move from their current home or condo are choosing to get everything they want and need out of a new construction project like Nautilus, without the bidding wars and slim availability that too often comes with resales. Real Estate is one of the only things that still appreciates with age and use (depending on the market).... so to purchase a unit at Nautilus while the value continues to appreciate (before anyone, including yourself, has ever lived there), is a smart investment and asset for yourself and your family.

If you aren't familiar with what makes this building so special, we’ve got you covered. Nautilus 220 truly embodies resort-style living and modern luxury, it has the best of both worlds with a unique combination of city and waterfront lifestyles. This luxe development is alongside an active 100-slip marina with easy inlet access for snorkeling, scuba diving, jet skiing, and every other water sport that floats your boat. You can enjoy Private Beach Club Access on Singer Island or the on-site Chef David Burke Waterfront Restaurant. The one-acre (yes we said one-acre) outdoor amenity deck overlooking the Marina, Intracoastal Waterway, and the Atlantic Ocean is the active adult’s dream getaway, with a resort-style pool (with beach entry and wading area), large multi-jet spa and gazebo, a putting green, sundeck with lounge chairs and cabanas, a sunrise Brazilian wood yoga deck and summer kitchen for grilling. Some other Nautilus 220 amenities include mini-bowling, billiards, card tables, a multipurpose soundproof studio for music and podcasts, a golf simulator, a theater lounge, a club room, and so much more. Nautilus 220 is comprised of 330 one-to-three-bedroom residences in two, 24-story towers ranging from 1,100 to 3,400 sq. ft. featuring various floor plans with unobstructed water views.

Buyer’s Agent, Jonathan Santiago says, “beyond the luxury appeal, buyers are attracted to the price point Nautilus is selling for compared to other New Construction projects on the water. While other waterfront projects in the area are starting in the multi-million dollar range, Nautilus is starting its prices at a significant decrease. This is what appeals to buyers looking for New Construction as an investment or permanent residence that would hold its value for the coming years.”

We know that New Construction is popping up everywhere between Miami and West Palm Beach and there is a lot to choose from. Whether it is sleek Modern homes or architecturally impressive condo buildings, there are a variety of homes to select from. Some people struggle with the choice of new construction over pre-owned properties. We personally love New Construction but understand that it may not be for everyone depending on budget, lifestyle, and the immediate need to move or not. title: Miami's Top 10 Best Date Night Spots
h1: Top 10 Best Date Night Spots in Miami
meta: Miami's Top 10 Best Date Night Spots. If you’re visiting or considering moving to the Miami area and looking for some insider tips from locals for that special date night spot, look no further!

blocks.text: Living in Miami is a lifestyle! If you’re visiting or considering moving to the Miami area and looking for some insider tips from locals for that special date night spot, look no further! We've got you covered! Take a journey on the waterside of the city and explore our top 10 places to relax and enjoy the company of your special someone.
1. Spa Day at Faena
Unwind with a couple's massage or spa treatment at the luxurious Faena Hotel. With its glamorous setting and excellent service, you and your date are sure to feel like royalty.
2. Eat at Komodo in Brickell
Sample some of Miami's best Asian cuisine at Komodo, located in the trendy Brickell neighborhood. The exquisite dishes and beautiful setting make for a perfect date night spot.
3. Beachwalk

Enjoy the gorgeous views of the Miami coastline as you walk, jog, skate or bike along the beach. This is a great way to get some exercise while spending time together. It’s Miami so every beach is beautiful but try the park settings of Lummus Park or Crandon Park.
4. Superblue Miami
Be transported to another world at Superblue Miami, an immersive art installation that is sure to dazzle and amaze. This is a perfect date night spot for those who appreciate art and creativity.
5. Rooftop Cinema Club

Catch a classic film or new release at the Rooftop Cinema Club, located at 1212 Lincoln Rd. With its gorgeous setting and great selection of films, this is sure to be a date night you'll never forget.
6. Fratelli la Bufala Cooking Class

For those with a culinary interest who love wine and Italian cuisine, try an interactive cooking date. Join the “Let’s Dough It” experience on Tuesday night where you will enjoy sipping wine and making woodfired pizza. Click HERE to sign up!
7. Wynwood Walls

Check out the amazing street art at Wynwood Walls, an outdoor gallery that is home to some of the best graffiti artists in the world. This is a perfect date night spot for those who appreciate art and culture.
8. South Beach

Take a stroll down what the locals call SoBe, one of Miami's most popular tourist beach destinations. With its white sand beaches and turquoise waters, this is a perfect spot to people watch and enjoy all things Miami.
9. Vizcaya Museum and Gardens

Explore the Vizcaya Museum and Gardens, a historic estate that is home to beautiful gardens, sculptures, and artwork. This is a great date night spot for history buffs or anyone who loves spending time outdoors.
10. Bal Harbour Shops

Browse the high-end shops at Bal Harbour Shops, where you can find everything from designer clothes to fine jewelry. This is a perfect date night spot for those who enjoy a little luxury in their life.
Live and Play in Miami

If you need other great ideas from a local, LiveModern would love to connect and help immerse you in all things Miami. We specialize in matching homebuyers with real estate based on their lifestyle, whether that includes boating, beachfront living, waterfront or downtown properties, entertainment, shopping, services, or just a night on the town.

title: The Best Golf Courses in Palm Beach County: Public and Private
h1: The Best Golf Courses in Palm Beach County: Public and Private
meta: Looking for the best golf courses in Palm Beach County? Read on to find the best public and private golf courses in Palm Beach County.

blocks.text: Golf is a beautiful and timeless game that has been enjoyed by people of all ages and skill levels for centuries. It is a sport that combines physical skill, mental strategy, and patience, and offers something for everyone. The origins of golf can be traced back to medieval Scotland, where it was played on rugged terrain using primitive equipment. Over time, the game evolved and became more organized, with the first official rules being written in 1744. Today, golf is played on carefully manicured courses that are designed to challenge players while also providing a beautiful and serene environment. The combination of the natural surroundings and expert course design make golf a truly timeless and delightful game. This is especially true in Palm Beach County, Florida. Home to some of the best golf courses in the world, this region offers a variety of public and private courses that are sure to challenge and delight all types of golfers. Whether you’re an experienced pro or just getting started with the game, here are some of the best public and private golf courses in Palm Beach County.
Private vs. Public: What’s the Difference?

When it comes to golf courses, there are two main types: private and public. Private courses require a paid membership and their access is limited to members and invited guests only. These courses are typically uncrowded, pristinely manicured, and accompanied by social activities and amenities. Public courses are open to anyone willing to pay for a round of golf and are usually less expensive than private courses.
The Best Private Golf Courses in Palm Beach County

The best private golf courses in Palm Beach offer a serene and unique golfing experience. These courses are designed to challenge players while also providing an aesthetically pleasing environment. The solitude and tranquility offered by private courses make them a great choice for golfers who want to focus on improving their game in an environment absent of the hustle and bustle of public courses. Check out our list of the top private golf courses in Palm Beach County.
Seminole (Juno Beach)

Designed by Donald Ross in 1929, Seminole Golf Club is a private golf club located in North Palm Beach, Florida. This timeless classic is consistently ranked as one of the best courses in the nation and offers 18 holes of championship golf. The course features rolling fairways, undulating greens, and strategic bunkering that will challenge even the best players.
The Bear’s Club (Jupiter)

Jack and Barbara Nicklaus founded The Bear’s Club in 1999 as a way to provide members and their guests with an unforgettable golf experience. At The Bear’s Club, Jack masterfully created a golf community of 369 pristine acres, just one mile from the Atlantic. His uncompromising standards have transformed this native environment into an 18-hole championship course spanning 7,164 yards across rolling landscapes blanketed with native vegetation; old oaks, pines, and cypress trees softened by wetlands areas to create a quintessential Lowcountry atmosphere. You won’t want to miss this incredible course when looking for the best private golf courses in Palm Beach County.
Jupiter Hills Club (Tequesta)

There’s just something about the Jupiter Hills Club that sets it apart from other private golf courses. Located in Martin County, the private Jupiter Hills Club is known for having some of the largest rolling hills for a South Florida golf course. Tequesta, Florida, is home to two remarkable 18-hole golf courses - the Hills Course and Village Course. The former was built in 1970 by George Fazio alongside his talented nephew Tom; the latter followed suit eight years later. While the Hills Course often takes center stage due to its high-quality design, don’t let that stop you from experiencing the excellence of Tequesta’s lesser-known but equally impressive village course! If you’re looking for an unforgettable round of golf with beautiful views, consider Jupiter Hills Club. It’s one of the best private golf courses in Palm Beach County.
The Best Public Golf Courses in Palm Beach County

While private golf courses have their advantages, there are plenty of excellent public courses in Palm Beach County as well. The best public courses in Palm Beach County provide a great golfing experience without the hefty price tag of private courses. These courses are designed to provide a variety of obstacles and challenges that help players improve their game while also providing a scenic atmosphere. If you’re looking for something more affordable and accessible, there are plenty of great public courses in Palm Beach County that you can enjoy. Here’s a selection of our favorites.
Osprey Point Golf Course (Boca Raton)

Designed as one of Florida’s premier public golf courses, Osprey Point has been lauded for its excellence since opening in 2010. This sprawling 27-hole course is separated into three separate nine holes and boasts a tranquil environment with lush Platinum Paspalum turf from tee to green. With such stunning playing surfaces available, you can be sure your next game will provide an unforgettable backdrop!
Southwinds Golf Club (Boca Raton)

If you seek a truly remarkable golfing experience, Southwinds Golf Club in Boca Raton is the perfect option. This 27-hole course provides an abundance of wildlife to observe, with foxes, Canadian geese, otters, and iguanas all making appearances throughout your round. Moreover, each hole features water, requiring a strategic shot placement so that you can make it around this serene setting without penalty strokes! Southwinds has proudly earned the prestigious title of Audubon International Cooperative Sanctuary, so you can rest assured that your round has a positive environmental impact!
Boca Dunes Golf & Country Club (Boca Raton)

Concluding our list is the remarkable Boca Dunes Golf & Country Club. This newly renovated course offers a dazzling 18-hole executive golf experience with eight par 4’s, ten par 3’s, and an unforgettable landscape featuring tif-eagle greens and continuous concrete cart paths. Boca Dunes is the ideal course for golfers of all skill sets, boasting a par 62 and extending over 3,600 yards from its furthest tees. If you’re searching for an unbeatable game in Palm Beach County, look no further than Boca Dunes! A perfect challenge awaits, with its generous layout sure to make your experience extraordinary.
Final Thoughts

Palm Beach County is home to some of the most beautiful and thrilling golf courses, both public and private. These courses feature breathtaking landscapes and difficult-hole designs that promise a truly special experience for all who visit them. So no matter what your preference may be, you are sure to find an exemplary course in Palm Beach County.

title: The Best Neighborhoods in Fort Lauderdale for an Active Lifestyle
h1: Best Fort Lauderdale Neighborhoods for an Active Lifestyle
meta: Looking to find the best areas to live in Fort Lauderdale that are ideal for an active lifestyle? Check out this blog post from Florida's leading real estate brokerage!

blocks.text: So you’re an adventurer. You love to explore new things, meet new people, and try new activities. You’re the type of person that wakes up early to go for a run on the beach, or takes an evening walk to explore the different neighborhoods in your city. You’re looking for a place to call home that will allow you to live an active lifestyle and never be bored.

If that sounds like you, then you’ll want to check out some of the nicest neighborhoods in Fort Lauderdale. You can find everything from beaches to tennis courts to kayaking and more. And of course, being in Florida, the weather is almost always perfect for outdoor activities.
So whether you’re looking for a place to retire or just want to find a new neighborhood to call home, here are some of the best areas to live in Fort Lauderdale for an active lifestyle.
Best Areas to Live in Fort Lauderdale for an Active Lifestyle
Las Olas

One of the most popular areas in Fort Lauderdale is Las Olas. This picturesque neighborhood is perfect for those that love to shop, dine, and be entertained. And, of course, it’s also great for those that love to live an active lifestyle.

As you make your way east of Las Olas Boulevard, you’ll find that shops start to decrease, and postcard-perfect views of yachts and mega-mansions begin to appear. It’s here where you’ll find some of the best running paths in the city, but you can also find plenty of places to go kayaking, paddle boarding, and more.

The 1.3 miles of Las Olas Blvd is a favorite among those who live here. Enjoy outdoor activities such as jogging, running, rollerblading, and biking, you’ll find dedicated bike lanes and plenty of green space.

Watch the sunset as you jog along A1A Fort Lauderdale Beach Park. Or take a leisurely stroll down Las Olas Riverwalk and enjoy the sights and sounds of the city. There’s always something active to do in Las Olas.
Riviera Isles

South of Las Olas Blvd and Sunset Lake, Riviera Isles is a luxurious neighborhood that’s perfect for those that love the water. This area is home to some of the most beautiful mega-mansions in the city, as well as world-class marinas.

If you love to sail, fish, or just enjoy being on the water, Riviera Isles is the place for you. There are plenty of charter companies that offer sailing excursions, and you can find some of the best fishing spots in the city.
With direct access to the Atlantic Ocean, you’ll never tire of the views in Riviera Isles. And you’ll also have easy access to all the best beaches in Fort Lauderdale.
The Fort Lauderdale Riverwalk

This pedestrian-only path is perfect for those that want to enjoy the outdoors without having to worry about traffic. The Riverwalk stretches for miles along the New River, making it one of the best Fort Lauderdale neighborhoods for an active lifestyle.

The path winds along both the south and north sides of the river, and there are plenty of places to stop and enjoy the views. You can also find restaurants, cafes, bars, and shops along the way. Shop for souvenirs, enjoy a bite to eat, and then continue on your journey.

Anointed “Florida’s Most Beautiful Mile,” the Riverwalk boasts tropical vegetation, luxurious yachts, high-rise towers, and plenty of photo-worthy views. Perfect for those that want to take a leisurely stroll or go for a vigorous run, the Riverwalk won’t disappoint when living in Fort Lauderdale.
Hollywood Beach

If you’re looking for a place to enjoy the sand and surf, Hollywood Beach is the perfect spot. With its famous boardwalk, expansive beaches, and plenty of activities, there’s always something to do in this vibrant beach town.

A one-of-a-kind destination, Hollywood Beach features a two-and-a-half-mile boardwalk that’s perfect for walking, jogging, biking, and rollerblading. You can also find plenty of shops, restaurants, cafes, and bars along the way.

And of course, no trip to Hollywood Beach would be complete without spending some time on the golden shore. If you’re looking for activities that’ll get your toes in the sand, try volleyball, beach tennis, paddleboarding, or kayaking.

Hollywood Beach doesn’t just offer sand and surf, but also a vibrant nightlife. When the sun goes down, the boardwalk comes alive with live music, street performers, and plenty of places to grab a bite or a drink.
Hollywood Beach is truly one of the nicest neighborhoods in Fort Lauderdale for those that want to enjoy an active lifestyle.
Victoria Park
If you’re looking for a more laid-back atmosphere, Victoria Park is the perfect place to call home. This historic neighborhood is known for its tree-lined streets and charming bungalows.

Despite its small-town feel, Victoria Park offers plenty of activities for those that want to stay active. There are plenty of parks to explore, as well as a golf course and tennis courts. You can also find a number of fitness studios and gyms in the area.
And if you’re looking for someplace to cool off, Victoria Park is home to The Victoria Park Pool. This wonderful spot features a heated lap pool, as well as a separate area for kids.
Victoria Park is one of the best Fort Lauderdale neighborhoods for families. With its excellent schools, lovely parks, and captivating bungalows, it’s easy to see why this neighborhood is so popular.
Get Active in Fort Lauderdale With LiveModern

No matter what kind of active lifestyle you’re looking for, you can find it in Fort Lauderdale. From the beaches to the Riverwalk to Victoria Park, there’s something for everyone in this beautiful city.
Living in Fort Lauderdale opens doors to a whole world of opportunity. And with LiveModern, you can find the perfect place in Fort Lauderdale to call home.

Whether you are looking for a condo, single family or townhome, we can help you find the perfect place in Fort Lauderdale and South Florida to live the active lifestyle you want to live. With plentiful knowledge of all that Fort Lauderdale has to offer, our agents provide the perfect basecamp for your next adventure.
Contact us today and discover the best that Fort Lauderdale has to offer. We can’t wait to show you around!

Forte on Flagler

Do you love waking up to the view of the water? How does a morning coffee and sunrise yoga sound? Amazing, right? Then Forte is for you. Forte is ideal for animal lovers, with a dog park to complete this oasis.

This is just a snapshot of some of the incredible changes happening to the West Palm Beach skyline, as more and more developers are investing in this ever-growing city and with so many beautiful buildings being constructed, it’s easy to see why.
South Flagler House

The construction-changing skyline of West Palm Beach doesn’t stop there. The breathtaking South Flagler House will have endless unobstructed views of the Intracoastal waterway, Worth Avenue, and the Atlantic Ocean.

South Flagler House will be popular with runners and those who love the outdoors, because of the location, making it the perfect destination for morning exercise. So if running along the intracoastal sounds like a dream for you, look no further.

This is one of the most sought-after and unique West Palm Beach properties. 41 residencies spread over 25 floors give the occupiers the perfect place to call home. However, the penthouse’s 24th floor is the real crown jewel in this new development, with 360-degree views from their new waterfront property’s floor-to-ceiling windows and an individual property that takes up the entire floor.
Embrace the tropical lifestyle with one of these beautiful new West Palm Beach condos; you won’t regret it!
Learn more about South Flagler House here!
Why Invest in West Palm Beach Condos & Houses for Sale?

West Palm Beach real estate sits alongside some of the best bars and restaurants, a world-renowned art museum, and broadway quality shows. The best neighborhoods in Palm Beach have everything you could ever want.

Known for its friendly beaches, epic sunsets, and piers ideal for fishing, West Palm Beach is perfect for families and couples. Clematis Street is known as West Palm Beach’s historic main street, while Antique Row along the Dixie Highway offers a unique experience for people who love to shop.

There are also plenty of activities for those who love the outdoors, with more than 30 public golf courses, many tennis courts, and parks to enjoy by yourself or with friends and family. title: The Top 6 New Technologies for the Boating Industry
h1: New Tech for the Boating Industry
meta: Learn about the top six new boating industry technologies that are shaking up the industry like never before.

blocks.text: There’s nothing quite like the feeling of being on the open water, with the waves ever-so-gently lapping against your boat. The wind in your hair, the sun on your face… it's an experience like no other. But if you’re a boating enthusiast, you know that there is always room for improvement in the industry.

The technological advances of the past few years have opened up new possibilities for recreational boaters and boat manufacturers alike. Think of electric outboard motors for super quiet boating, co-pilot navigational systems that use AI to help you reach your destination safely, and green technologies meant to reduce the carbon footprint of ocean vessels.

The evolution of the boating industry will emerge with the introduction of new and innovative technologies that will bring a whole range of benefits to boaters. Here are six top boating industry trends you should be aware of.
6 Boating Industry Trends
1. Go Green or Go Home

With the world becoming more eco-friendly and conscious of carbon emissions, boating technology has also taken a green turn. Hybrid propulsion systems are becoming increasingly popular as they use gasoline and electric power to run yachts. This is an important step for reducing the overall carbon footprint of ocean vessels, as well as creating a more sustainable boating experience.

Moreover, solar energy is no longer just helping us power our homes but solar and wind power are quickly becoming viable sources of power for boats. According to the International Maritime Organization, these green technologies use renewable energy sources that can help reduce your boat’s reliance on fossil fuels, which contribute to 1.12 billion tons of CO₂ emissions every year.
2. Take Your Boating to the Next Level with AI and Automation

The introduction of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and automation into boating technology have revolutionized how boats are operated. For example, with co-pilot navigational systems, you can navigate your boat using a virtual map of your destination and get real-time updates on conditions. This helps you steer clear of obstacles and stay safe on the water.

AI has made autopilot features possible as well. Autopilots are computerized programs that work in combination with onboard navigation systems to control the boat’s direction, speed, and depth. It can help increase safety by allowing you to take your hands off the wheel while still being able to steer in a precise manner.
3. Enjoy Silence with Electric Outboard Motors
Electric outboard motors are now available, which offer impressive performance but without all the noise.

Electric outboard motors are the way of the future for smaller boats. They offer amazing performance and power, but without all the racket and fuel consumption of traditional gasoline engines. We’re already seeing some of the most advanced electric outboard motors, like Torqeedo 1103C, that even come with GPS-based range calculation.

The Torqeedo motor is one of the few that offers such capabilities, and it’s a great way to take your boating experience up a notch, while keeping the noise down. Enjoy being on the water without disturbing the marine life around you!
4. Robotics and 3D Printing
If we would’ve told you a decade ago that one day robots would be used in the boating industry, you’d probably laugh. But that is now a reality.

Robots are being used for a variety of tasks on boats, from basic maintenance to complex repairs. They can even help with dangerous and difficult-to-reach areas like underwater hull inspections. In addition, risky and costly tasks like firefighting can be conducted with robots instead of humans. Sensors are also helping to identify and record all the data in a ship, from fuel levels to structural integrity.

Another interesting trend that’s slowly making its way into the boating industry is 3D printing. It allows for the creation of complicated parts which would be impossible to make through traditional manufacturing methods. This makes it ideal for boats, as many of their components involve complex shapes and designs that are difficult to craft.

Overall, robotics and 3D printing are two key boating industry trends that we’ll be seeing a lot more of in the near future. They could revolutionize how boats are built and maintained, making them safer and easier to manage.
5. Let’s Get Digital
Can you imagine operating a boat without having to use physical controls or dials? That’s exactly what digital transformation has enabled in the boating industry.

Multi-service apps are taking the place of traditional Crestron remote control units, allowing clients and crew to easily control the yacht’s lighting, climate, and entertainment systems with their smartphones. This is made possible through high-bandwidth connectivity enabled by the advancement of satellite and wireless technologies.
With a digital transformation, you can reduce operational costs, increase efficiency, and make your boating experience more enjoyable.
6. Safety First
We know that it’s pretty ironic we put safety last on this list, but it’s still one of the most important aspects to consider when boating.
Fatalities due to boating accidents have been steadily increasing, which why it’s important to ensure your boat is properly equipped with the latest safety devices.

The new Fleet Safety software can help yacht owners and operators improve their vessels’ safety by providing them with vital information on boating industry trends, boating industry technology, and safety plans and procedures.

Its instant distress chat feature also connects users with over 50 Maritime Rescue Coordination Centers, which can save up to 20 minutes in search and rescue operations. A black box can also be used to investigate accidents and potentially save lives.
This is a pretty big deal for the boating industry.
The Conclusion
So there you have it—six cutting-edge boating trends that will make your experience even more enjoyable and safe.

The boating industry is changing rapidly, so it’s important to stay up-to-date with the latest trends and technologies. In addition, keeping your boat well-equipped with the right safety devices can help you have a stress-free time on the water whether you're sailing in our home, South Florida or anywhere the seas may take you.

Some of the above boating industry trends have already made their way onto boats, while others are still in the early stages. But, of course, this is just the tip of the iceberg, so keep your eyes peeled for more boating innovations!

title: Top 5 Miami Condo Buildings We Love for Their Interiors
h1: Top 5 Miami Condo Buildings We Love for Their Interiors
meta: Five of the best condo buildings we love for their interiors and overall living experience.

blocks.text: The Magic City is known to embody everything that makes a good life. There are culturally forward restaurants opening up at every corner, over-the-top events that attract global, high-power audiences, and endless miles of pristine beaches beckoning visitors near and far.

It’s no surprise more out-of-towners are laying roots in South Florida. The Miami metro population grew to 6,215,000 residents—a .78 percent increase from last year alone. And they’re not all here to snap up real estate in the traditional way of buying a home. Condos and designer residences have been a hot commodity in the real estate market, with more than 52 (and counting) new and relaunched condo and residence projects announced since last year.

With so many condos going up, it can become a little overwhelming to know which one is the right fit. Lucky for you, we’ve rounded up five of the best condo buildings we love for their interiors and overall living experience. Read on to learn about these standout properties.
1. Paramount World Center

Standing just over 700 feet, Paramount World Center is hard to not miss in downtown Miami’s skyline. The soaring curvilinear glass tower was completed in 2019 and features 569 condo residences spread across 58 stories comprised of studios, one-, two-, and three-bedroom floor plans, in addition to luxe penthouses. Elkus Manfredi Architects and ID & Design International took on this project, which is certified LEED Silver (the most widely used green building system in the world).

Notes of interest include private elevator access with private foyers, 10-foot ceilings, outdoor living rooms with panoramic city views, designer European kitchens, rain showers, and more. The building’s upper deck is home to incredible amenities such as poolside bungalows and daybeds, a summer kitchen with a barbecue, a soccer field, tennis courts, and a jogging path. In total, Paramount is the ultimate spot to call home in Miami.
2. Residences by Armani Casa

Those looking to live the ultimate designer lifestyle can call Residences by Armani Casa home. This Sunny Isles crown jewel is sleek and modern, with breathtaking waterfront views for anyone who yearns for oceanfront living. Architect Cesar Pelli worked alongside designer Giorgio Armani Casa to bring this 56-story property to life in 2019. In short, amenities feature concierge and security, a full-service restaurant, spa and fitness center, an expansive pool, and access to some of Miami’s best shopping destinations, such as Bal Harbour Shops and Aventura Mall.

Amenities aside, the real show-stopper at this property is the interiors. According to the website, designer Armani Casa says the project was born from his desire to see his design aesthetic at work in interior spaces. “We get the opportunity to create something very special—a remarkable living space imbued with the spirit of modern elegance,” he says.

Gold, bronze, and green shades are visible in common areas, such as the curved entry. Residences range from two- to four-bedroom floor plans, all designed with modern appliances and floor-to-ceiling glass. Armani/Dada, a joint brand of the Italian design group Molteni&C and Armani, created the cabinetry in the kitchen. Master bedrooms feature a marble sink, and the bathrooms are finished with a tub designed by Armani/Roca, a team effort between the Roca Group and Armani.
3. One Thousand Museum

One of downtown Miami’s most iconic buildings to date is thanks to the late architect Zaha Hadid. One Thousand Museum is a 62-story building in front of Museum Park, close to the Pérez Art Museum and Patricia and Phillip Frost Museum of Science. A standout feature of this skyscraper is the “exoskeleton” that curves up the sides of the recessed glass facades, which ultimately keeps floor plans free of internal columns.

Hadid also designed the interiors, which feature her signature angular walls and curved surfaces. She also partnered with G.T. Design to create bespoke flooring designs and rugs in the common areas and apartments. Hadid tapped ITALKRAFT as a partner to deliver “world-class gourmet kitchens, dream closets, and designer bathrooms.” Each unit features Italian-crafted kitchens finished with matte white lacquer and dark Italian cabinetry, natural wood furnishings, and spacious designer closets.
4. The Estates at Aqualina

At the intersection of design and technology is The Estates of Aqualina on beautiful Sunny Isles Beach. Featuring 248 residences and four- to seven-bedroom floor plans across two 50-story towers, this residency is the epitome of luxe living. It’s no surprise the developers called upon Chanel’s creative director Karl Lagerfeld to design two residential lobby spaces and their custom features, finishes, and furnishings, marking his first interior project in the U.S.

Each estate comes furniture-ready and equipped with the finest appliances, imported stone countertops, smart-home technology, and 10-foot ceilings. According to its website, the estates’ master suites boast his and her bathroom suites with imported onyx countertops and walls plus marble floors, a free-standing tub, an ocean-view shower, and more.
5. Porsche Design Tower

Luxury and elegance intersect with convenience and innovation inside another stunner in Sunny Isles: Porsche Design Tower. Soaring 60 stories high, Porsche Design Tower is one of the city’s tallest buildings at 641 feet. Its 132 residential units are all equipped with the latest and greatest technology you’d imagine for the price tag. Of note is the signature Dezervator (named after the tower’s developer, Gil Dezer), a one-of-a-kind elevator. The car elevator system was created to allow residents to not have to go to a garage to get their car or wait on valet—a bonus for high-profile guests. Instead, owners can drive their vehicle onto a turntable, which takes them to their private garage. Three Dezervators are on the property, all visible from the lobby.

Each condo offers a relaxing escape with a pool on every resident’s balcony, plus large terraces and summer kitchens to enjoy killer oceanfront views. Residences are equipped with floor-to-ceiling glass windows, a Poggenpohl-designed kitchen with motorized doors and an expansive island, granite or marble countertops in the kitchen and bathrooms, Dornbracht fixtures in the kitchen and master bathroom, and private interior elevator in the duplex residences.
For more information on the above condos or to check out even more options, head to Live Modern to find your new forever home.

blocks.text: Picture this; you wake up and spend your entire morning on the water. You paddle board, go for a swim, and lounge on your yacht as you enjoy the rays with your significant other. As the sun begins to set, your stomach starts to growl. But you haven’t had enough of the clear blue water just yet. You want to watch the sun dip below the horizon while enjoying a delicious meal!

The Best Waterfront Restaurants in Miami

Now that you know a little bit more about our luxury real estate services, let’s get to the good stuff - the food! Miami is home to some seriously scrumptious dishes, and there’s no better place to enjoy it than with a glorious waterfront view.
Whether you’re in the mood for fresh sushi, traditional Argentinian cuisine, or something in between, we’ve got you covered. Check out our list of the best waterfront restaurants in Miami below.
1. La Mar by Gaston Acurio

Have you ever tried world-renowned Peruvian cuisine? Your first time will be a charm with La Mar by Gaston Acurio. This award-winning restaurant is located in the Mandarin Oriental hotel on Brickell Key and offers stunning views of both the Miami skyline and Biscayne Bay.

Chef Acurio is a celebrity in the culinary world, and his restaurant does not disappoint. From causas (potato dishes) and ceviches to tiraditos (Peruvian sashimi) and anticuchos (skewers), there’s something for everyone at La Mar. And don’t even get us started on the pisco cocktails...

Take the love of your life out for a romantic dinner, or bring the whole family for a night they won’t soon forget at this top waterfront restaurant in Miami. Just be sure to make a reservation - both their indoor and outdoor seating is always packed!
2. Joia Beach
If you’re in the mood for something a little more laid back, head over to Joia Beach. This open-air seafood spot is located right on the sand in Miami Beach and offers beautiful oceanfront views.

Joia Beach serves up fresh, simple seafood dishes with an Italian twist. Their menu changes seasonally to ensure that they’re always using the freshest ingredients, but some of their staples include the homemade gnocchi, wood-fired octopus, and wild-caught fish.
Whether you want to enjoy a leisurely lunch with your toes in the sand or watch the sunset with a glass of wine in hand, Joia Beach is the unapologetic pleasure of Miami’s waterfront dining culture.
3. Tigre
For a taste of Latin America, look no further than Tigre. This Argentinian-style churrascaria is nestled on the banks of a manatee sanctuary, close to Biscayne Boulevard.

The serenity of river life combined with fresh, wood-fired fare makes Tigre a truly unique dining experience. Their menu features traditional Argentinian dishes like red prawn tiradito, pulpo and papitas, and of course, churrasco.

While the menu’s larger plates skew toward sea bass and steak, it also offers a variety of small dishes, many of which are based on Argentinian classics with an elevated creative approach. There are vegetable-forward meals as well as grilled meat and plenty of seafood on offer.
Don’t miss out on this one-of-a-kind spot the next time you’re looking for the top waterfront restaurants in Miami!
4. Baia Beach Club Restaurant
Elegance is an understatement at Baia Beach Club Restaurant.

Baia nourishes the effortless elegance of international coastal living to the contemporary and reinvented Mondrian South Beach. It is truly a world-class destination in its own right, just waiting to be discovered.

Inspired by a love for the Mediterranean sea, the laid-back bohemian energy, casual chic, and warm hospitality of the Miami Beach lifestyle, the menu at Baia features refined yet approachable fare with an emphasis on fresh seafood and raw bar items. The result is a modern twist on classic beach club dining that feels both luxurious and relaxed.

Located just steps from the sand, Baia’s outdoor terrace is the perfect place to enjoy a meal with your significant other or your closest friends. And with the Miami skyline as your backdrop, you’ll be hard-pressed to find a more beautiful spot.

title: Unlock the Power of the Sun: Harnessing Solar Energy For Homeowners
h1: Unlock the Power of the Sun: Harnessing Solar Energy For Homeowners
meta: Discover more about solar panels for your home and what to consider before making the investment of a solar panel roofing system for your house.

blocks.text: Solar energy is one of the most promising forms of renewable energy available today. Not only is it a clean and environmentally-friendly source of power, but it also has the potential to save homeowners a lot of money on their electricity bills, while increasing the overall value of their property.

From the best solar panels for homes to entire solar systems for houses, there are many ways to make your home more energy-efficient and reduce your electricity costs. In this article, we’ll discuss the basics of solar power, identify different types of solar panels best suited for homes, and provide tips on how to best utilize solar energy for your home.
What are Solar Panels for Homeowners? How Do They Work?

Solar panels are an efficient way to convert the sun’s energy into usable electricity. These innovative devices, constructed with small cells made of semiconductors, such as silicon, capture and transform sunlight into direct current (DC). To utilize this DC within a home, an inverter is necessary for it to be converted into alternating current (AC), allowing any appliance in the vicinity access to its energy source. Solar panels for homeowners are becoming an increasingly popular option for those looking to reduce their energy bills and their carbon footprint.
Benefits of Solar Power for Homeowners

The benefits of solar power may vary for homeowners. The degree of savings and other benefits can change depending on the type of system installed. Not only does utilizing a solar system for homes bring about considerable financial rewards, such as decreased electricity bills and increased property values, but it also has the capacity to reduce your environmental footprint. Let’s explore some of the most beneficial aspects that come with embracing solar energy for homeowners.
Types of Solar Panels for Homeowners

With a variety of solar panel models available, from traditional monocrystalline and multicrystalline to cutting-edge options such as solar shingle roofing systems or building-integrated photovoltaics (BIPV), it’s essential to consider the regional environment, number of hours with direct sunlight, and any other particular requirements before selecting panels for your residence. Every type comes with its own advantages and disadvantages; hence you must be aware of all these factors when making an informed decision.
Here are some of the most common types of solar energy for homeowners:

With the correct insight and instruction, selecting the ideal solar panels for your home is a breeze! Solar energy is an eco-friendly and sustainable source of power which may drastically reduce your utility bill, lower your carbon footprint, and provide a world of other advantages.
Tips on How to Best Utilize Solar Energy for Your Home
Perform a Solar Assessment

Make sure you have the best possible understanding of what type of solar installation will best suit your residence by having an assessment performed by a qualified professional. They can provide valuable advice on the best type of solar panels to install, based on your roofing structure, climate requirements, and other factors.
Shop Around for Solar Financing

Installing a solar energy system is an investment that requires careful consideration when it comes to financing. Before committing to any particular option, make sure you shop around and compare rates and conditions between multiple lenders.
Take Advantage of Incentives and Rebates

In addition to shopping for the best financing option, make sure you know about all potential incentives available for solar installations in your area. From federal tax credits to state-level rebates and local utility company discounts, these incentives can significantly reduce the cost of your installation.
Don’t Skip Maintenance

Lastly, don’t forget about maintenance. Even though solar panel installation is a relatively low-maintenance setup, it’s best to schedule regular cleanings and inspections with qualified professionals to ensure optimal performance throughout its lifetime.
Final Thoughts

Solar energy is an increasingly viable home solution for many homeowners today due to the numerous benefits it offers. With the right information and proper setup, you can make sure that your house is best utilizing this renewable energy source and enjoying all its advantages!

This article has provided some insight into the best solar panels for homes, and entire solar systems for houses. At LiveModern, we understand the benefits of renewable energy and are passionate about helping homeowners find the best homes and resources to suite their lifestyle.
